items.title: DCP: Addressing Input Dynamism In Long-Context Training via Dynamic Context Parallelism.
items.abstract: Context parallelism has emerged as a key technique to support long-context training, a growing trend in generative AI for modern large models. However, existing context parallel methods rely on static parallelization configurations that overlook the dynamic nature of training data, specifically, the variability in sequence lengths and token relationships (i.e., attention patterns) across samples. As a result, these methods often suffer from unnecessary communication overhead and imbalanced computation. In this paper, we present DCP, a dynamic context parallel training framework that introduces fine-grained blockwise partitioning of both data and computation. By enabling flexible mapping of data and computation blocks to devices, DCP can adapt to varying sequence characteristics, effectively reducing communication and improving memory and computation balance. Micro-benchmarks demonstrate that DCP accelerates attention by 1.19x~2.45x under causal masks and 2.15x~3.77x under sparse attention patterns. Additionally, we observe up to 0.94x~1.16x end-to-end training speed-up for causal masks, and 1.00x~1.46x for sparse masks.
items.abstract_zh: 上下文并行性已成为支持长上下文训练的关键技术，长上下文训练是现代大型模型的生成人工智能的增长趋势。然而，现有的上下文并行方法依赖于静态并行化配置，忽略了训练数据的动态性质，特别是样本之间序列长度和标记关系（即注意力模式）的可变性。因此，这些方法经常遭受不必要的通信开销和不平衡的计算。在本文中，我们提出了 DCP，一种动态上下文并行训练框架，它引入了数据和计算的细粒度分块分区。通过实现数据和计算块到设备的灵活映射，DCP可以适应不同的序列特征，有效减少通信并改善内存和计算平衡。微基准测试表明，DCP 在因果掩模下将注意力加速了 1.19 倍~2.45 倍，在稀疏注意力模式下加速了 2.15 倍~3.77 倍。此外，我们观察到因果掩码的端到端训练加速高达 0.94 倍~1.16 倍，稀疏掩码的端到端训练加速为 1.00 倍~1.46 倍。

items.title: Fast End-to-End Performance Simulation of Accelerated Hardware-Software Stacks.
items.abstract:
The increased use of hardware acceleration has created a need for efficient simulators of the end-to-end performance of accelerated hardware-software stacks: both software and hardware developers need to evaluate the impact of their design choices on overall system performance. However, accurate full-stack simulations are extremely slow, taking hours to simulate just 1 second of real execution. As a result, development of accelerated stacks is non-interactive, and this hurts productivity.

We propose a way to simulate end-to-end performance that is orders-of-magnitude faster yet still accurate. The main idea is to take a minimalist approach: We simulate only those components of the system that are not available, and run the rest natively. Even for unavailable components, we simulate cycle-accurately only aspects that are performance-critical. The key challenge is how to correctly and efficiently synchronize the natively executing components with the simulated ones.

Using this approach, we demonstrate 6× to 879× speedup compared to the state of the art, across three different hardware-accelerated stacks. The accuracy of simulated time is high: 7% error rate on average and 14% in the worst case, assuming CPU cores are not underprovisioned. Reducing simulation time down to seconds enables interactive development of accelerated stacks, which was until now not possible.
items.abstract_zh:
硬件加速使用的增加产生了对加速硬件-软件堆栈的端到端性能的高效模拟器的需求：软件和硬件开发人员都需要评估其设计选择对整体系统性能的影响。然而，准确的全栈模拟速度非常慢，需要几个小时才能模拟仅 1 秒的实际执行。因此，加速堆栈的开发是非交互式的，这会损害生产力。

我们提出了一种模拟端到端性能的方法，该方法速度快几个数量级，但仍然准确。主要思想是采用极简方法：我们仅模拟系统中不可用的组件，并在本地运行其余组件。即使对于不可用的组件，我们也仅对性能关键的方面进行精确的周期模拟。关键的挑战是如何正确有效地同步本机执行组件与模拟组件。

使用这种方法，我们在三个不同的硬件加速堆栈上展示了与现有技术相比 6 倍到 879 倍的加速。模拟时间的准确性很高：假设 CPU 核心没有配置不足，平均错误率为 7%，最坏情况下为 14%。将仿真时间减少到几秒可以实现加速堆栈的交互式开发，而这在以前是不可能的。

items.title: eBPF Misbehavior Detection: Fuzzing with a Specification-Based Oracle.
items.abstract:
Bugs in the Linux eBPF verifier may cause it to mistakenly accept unsafe eBPF programs or reject safe ones, causing either security or usability issues. While prior works on fuzzing the eBPF verifier have been effective, their bug oracles only hint at the existence of bugs indirectly ( e.g. , when a memory error occurs in downstream execution) instead of showing the root cause, confining them to uncover a narrow range of security bugs only with no detection of usability issues.

In this paper, we propose SpecCheck, a specification-based oracle integrated with our fuzzer Veritas, to detect a wide range of bugs in the eBPF verifier. SpecCheck encodes eBPF instruction semantics and safety properties as a specification and turns the claim of whether a concrete eBPF program is safe into checking the satisfiability of the corresponding safety constraints, which can be reasoned automatically without abstraction. The output from the oracle will be crosschecked with the eBPF verifier for any discrepancies. Using SpecCheck, Veritas uncovered 13 bugs in the Linux eBPF verifier, including severe bugs that can cause privilege escalation or information leakage, as well as bugs that cause frustration in even experienced kernel developers.
items.abstract_zh:
Linux eBPF 验证器中的错误可能会导致其错误地接受不安全的 eBPF 程序或拒绝安全的程序，从而导致安全或可用性问题。虽然之前对 eBPF 验证器进行模糊测试的工作是有效的，但他们的 bug 预言只是间接暗示 bug 的存在（例如，当下游执行中发生内存错误时），而不是显示根本原因，从而限制了他们仅发现一小部分安全 bug，而没有检测到可用性问题。

在本文中，我们提出了 SpecCheck，这是一种与我们的模糊器 Veritas 集成的基于规范的预言机，用于检测 eBPF 验证器中的各种错误。 SpecCheck将eBPF指令语义和安全属性编码为规范，并将具体eBPF程序是否安全的声明转化为检查相应安全约束的可满足性，无需抽象即可自动推理。预言机的输出将与 eBPF 验证器交叉检查是否存在任何差异。 Veritas 使用 SpecCheck 发现了 Linux eBPF 验证程序中的 13 个错误，其中包括可能导致权限升级或信息泄漏的严重错误，以及甚至会让经验丰富的内核开发人员感到沮丧的错误。

items.title: Aeolia: A Fast and Secure Userspace Interrupt-Based Storage Stack.
items.abstract:
Polling-based userspace storage stacks achieve great I/O performance. However, they cannot efficiently and securely share disks and CPUs among multiple tasks. In contrast, interrupt-based kernel stacks inherently suffer from subpar I/O performance but achieve advantages in resource sharing.

We present Aeolia, a novel storage stack that achieves great I/O performance while offering efficient and secure resource sharing. Aeolia is an interrupt-based userspace storage stack, representing a new point in the design space previously considered unfeasible. Our main observation is that, contrary to conventional wisdom, polling offers only marginal disk performance improvements over interrupts. Aeolia exploits user interrupt, an emerging hardware feature commonly used for userspace IPIs, in a novel way to deliver storage interrupts directly to userspace, thereby achieving high I/O performance with direct access. Aeolia leverages the hardware intra-process isolation features and sched_ext, an eBPF-based userspace scheduling framework, to efficiently and securely share CPUs and disks among multiple tasks, challenging the common belief that these are inherent disadvantages of userspace storage stacks. The above design enables Aeolia to realize AeoFS, a high-performance library file system that securely and directly accesses disks. Our evaluation shows that Aeolia outperforms Linux by 2× and AeoFS outperforms ext4 by up to 19.1×, respectively.
items.abstract_zh:
基于轮询的用户空间存储堆栈实现了出色的 I/O 性能。但是，它们无法在多个任务之间高效、安全地共享磁盘和 CPU。相比之下，基于中断的内核堆栈本质上会受到 I/O 性能不佳的影响，但在资源共享方面具有优势。

我们推出 Aeolia，一种新颖的存储堆栈，可实现出色的 I/O 性能，同时提供高效、安全的资源共享。 Aeolia 是一个基于中断的用户空间存储堆栈，代表了以前认为不可行的设计空间中的一个新点。我们的主要观察结果是，与传统观点相反，轮询仅比中断提供了边际的磁盘性能改进。 Aeolia 利用用户中断（一种常用于用户空间 IPI 的新兴硬件功能）以一种新颖的方式将存储中断直接传递到用户空间，从而通过直接访问实现高 I/O 性能。 Aeolia 利用硬件进程内隔离功能和基于 eBPF 的用户空间调度框架 sched_ext 在多个任务之间高效、安全地共享 CPU 和磁盘，挑战了人们普遍认为这些是用户空间存储堆栈固有缺点的观点。上述设计使Aeolia能够实现AeoFS这一安全、直接访问磁盘的高性能库文件系统。我们的评估表明，Aeolia 的性能优于 Linux 2 倍，AeoFS 的性能优于 ext4 高达 19.1 倍。

items.title: Managing Scalable Direct Storage Accesses for GPUs with GoFS.
items.abstract:
As we shift from CPU-centric computing to GPU-accelerated computing for supporting intelligent data processing at scale, the storage bottleneck has been exacerbated. To bypass the host CPUand alleviate unnecessary data movements, modern GPUs enable direct storage access to SSDs (i.e., GPUDirect Storage). However, current GPUDirect Storage solutions still rely on the host file system to manage the storage device, direct storage accesses are still bottlenecked by the host.

In this paper, we develop a G PU- o rchestrated f ile s ystem (GoFS) for scaling the direct storage accesses for GPU programs, by fully offloading the storage management to the GPU. As GoFS provides POSIX API and manages core filesystem structures in GPU memory, it can execute both control path and data path without host CPU involvement. To enable highly concurrent direct storage accesses, we rethink the design and implementation of core filesystem structures with various optimization techniques, such as scalable data indexing, fine-grained per-SM (streaming multiprocessor) block management, and zero-copy I/O accesses, by carefully exploring the GPU-accelerated computing paradigm. GoFS preserves the essential filesystem properties such as crash consistency, and it is compatible with existing host-based file systems like F2FS. GoFS does not require changes to the on-disk filesystem organization, therefore, the host and GPU can manage the SSD in a coordinated fashion, and maintain the data consistency in a primary/secondary mode. We implement GoFS based on F2FS using 7.9K lines of codes with CUDA programming. We examine its efficiency on an A100 GPU. Our experiments with various GPU-based applications show that GoFS outperforms state-of-the-art storage access solutions for GPUs by 1.61× on average.
items.abstract_zh:
随着我们从以 CPU 为中心的计算转向以 GPU 加速的计算来支持大规模智能数据处理，存储瓶颈已经加剧。为了绕过主机 CPU 并减少不必要的数据移动，现代 GPU 启用了对 SSD 的直接存储访问（即 GPU 直接存储）。然而，目前的GPUDirect Storage解决方案仍然依赖主机文件系统来管理存储设备，直接存储访问仍然受到主机的瓶颈。

在本文中，我们开发了一种 GPU 协调文件系统（GoFS），通过将存储管理完全卸载到 GPU 来扩展 GPU 程序的直接存储访问。由于 GoFS 提供 POSIX API 并管理 GPU 内存中的核心文件系统结构，因此它可以执行控制路径和数据路径，而无需主机 CPU 的参与。为了实现高度并发的直接存储访问，我们通过仔细探索 GPU 加速计算范例，重新思考核心文件系统结构的设计和实现，采用各种优化技术，例如可扩展数据索引、细粒度每 SM（流式多处理器）块管理和零复制 I/O 访问。 GoFS 保留了基本的文件系统属性，例如崩溃一致性，并且与 F2FS 等现有的基于主机的文件系统兼容。 GoFS不需要改变磁盘上的文件系统组织，因此主机和GPU可以协调管理SSD，并以主从模式保持数据一致性。我们使用 7.9K 行代码并通过 CUDA 编程实现了基于 F2FS 的 GoFS。我们在 A100 GPU 上检查其效率。我们对各种基于 GPU 的应用程序进行的实验表明，GoFS 的性能平均比最先进的 GPU 存储访问解决方案高出 1.61 倍。

items.title: Fawkes: Finding Data Durability Bugs in DBMSs via Recovered Data State Verification.
items.abstract:
Data durability is a fundamental requirement in DBMSs, ensuring that committed data remains intact despite unexpected faults such as power failures. Despite its critical importance, implementations of durability and recovery mechanisms continue to exhibit flaws, leading to severe issues(e.g., data loss, data inconsistency), which we refer to as Data Durability Bugs (DDBs). However, there is a limited understanding of the characteristics and root causes of DDBs. Furthermore, existing testing methods(e.g., Mallory) are often inadequate for detecting DDBs, particularly those that cause data loss or data inconsistency following DBMS failures.

This paper presents a comprehensive study of 43 DDBs across four widely used DBMSs. It reveals that DDBs primarily manifest as data loss, data inconsistency, log corruption, and system unavailability, often stem from flawed durability and recovery mechanisms, and are typically triggered when faults occur during filesystem or kernel-level calls. Based on these findings, we developed Fawkes, a testing framework to detect DDBs with recovered data state verification. It employs context-aware fault injection to target critical filesystem and kernel-level regions, functionality-guided fault triggering to explore untested paths, and checkpoint-based data graph verification to detect post-crash inconsistencies. We applied Fawkes to eight popular DBMSs and discovered 48 previously unknown DDBs, of which 16 have been fixed and 8 have been assigned CVE identifiers due to the severity.
items.abstract_zh:
数据持久性是 DBMS 的基本要求，确保提交的数据在出现意外故障（例如电源故障）时保持完整。尽管其至关重要，但持久性和恢复机制的实现仍然存在缺陷，导致严重的问题（例如数据丢失、数据不一致），我们将其称为数据持久性错误（DDB）。然而，人们对 DDB 的特征和根本原因了解有限。此外，现有的测试方法（例如 Mallory）通常不足以检测 DDB，特别是那些在 DBMS 故障后导致数据丢失或数据不一致的方法。

本文对四种广泛使用的 DBMS 中的 43 个 DDB 进行了全面研究。它揭示了 DDB 主要表现为数据丢失、数据不一致、日志损坏和系统不可用，通常源于有缺陷的持久性和恢复机制，并且通常在文件系统或内核级调用期间发生故障时触发。基于这些发现，我们开发了 Fawkes，这是一个测试框架，用于检测具有恢复数据状态验证的 DDB。它采用上下文感知故障注入来定位关键文件系统和内核级区域，采用功能引导的故障触发来探索未经测试的路径，并采用基于检查点的数据图验证来检测崩溃后的不一致。我们将 Fawkes 应用于 8 个流行的 DBMS，发现了 48 个以前未知的 DDB，其中 16 个已修复，8 个由于严重性已被分配 CVE 标识符。

items.title: CHERIoT RTOS: An OS for Fine-Grained Memory-Safe Compartments on Low-Cost Embedded Devices.
items.abstract:
Embedded systems do not benefit from strong memory protection, because they are designed to minimize cost. At the same time, there is increasing pressure to connect embedded devices to the internet, where their vulnerable nature makes them routinely subject to compromise. This fundamental tension leads to the current status-quo where exploitable devices put individuals and critical infrastructure at risk.

We present the design of a dependable embedded OS where compartmentalization and memory safety are first-class citizens. We co-design the OS with an embedded hardware platform that implements CHERI capabilities at a similar cost profile to existing chips with minimal security. We demonstrate key design benefits: fine-grained fault-tolerant compartments, OS-level support for compartment-interface hardening, and auditing facilities to thwart supply-chain attacks, among others, and show that they come at a memory usage and performance cost that allows their widespread deployment in cheap, resource-constrained devices.
items.abstract_zh:
嵌入式系统无法从强大的内存保护中受益，因为它们的设计目的是最大限度地降低成本。与此同时，将嵌入式设备连接到互联网的压力越来越大，它们的脆弱性使它们经常受到损害。这种根本性的紧张局势导致了当前的现状，可利用的设备使个人和关键基础设施面临风险。

我们提出了可靠的嵌入式操作系统的设计，其中分区和内存安全是一流的。我们与嵌入式硬件平台共同设计操作系统，该平台以与现有芯片相似的成本配置实现 CHERI 功能，且安全性最低。我们展示了关键的设计优势：细粒度的容错隔间、对隔间接口强化的操作系统级支持以及阻止供应链攻击的审核设施等，并表明它们的内存使用和性能成本允许它们在廉价、资源受限的设备中广泛部署。

items.title: ORQ: Complex Analytics on Private Data with Strong Security Guarantees.
items.abstract:
We present Orq, a system that enables collaborative analysis of large private datasets using cryptographically secure multi-party computation (MPC). Orq protects data against semi-honest or malicious parties and can efficiently evaluate relational queries with multi-way joins and aggregations that have been considered notoriously expensive under MPC. To do so, Orq eliminates the quadratic cost of secure joins by leveraging the fact that, in practice, the structure of many real queries allows us to join records and apply the aggregations "on the fly" while keeping the result size bounded. On the system side, Orq contributes generic oblivious operators, a data-parallel vectorized query engine, a communication layer that amortizes MPC network costs, and a dataflow API for expressing relational analytics—all built from the ground up.

We evaluate Orq in LAN and WAN deployments on a diverse set of workloads, including complex queries with multiple joins and custom aggregations. When compared to state-of-the-art solutions, Orq significantly reduces MPC execution times and can process one order of magnitude larger datasets. For our most challenging workload, the full TPC-H benchmark, we report results entirely under MPC with Scale Factor 10—a scale that had previously been achieved only with information leakage or the use of trusted compute.
items.abstract_zh:
我们推出了 Orq，这是一个能够使用加密安全多方计算 (MPC) 对大型私有数据集进行协作分析的系统。 Orq 可以保护数据免受半诚实或恶意方的侵害，并且可以通过多路联接和聚合有效地评估关系查询，而这些在 MPC 下被认为是非常昂贵的。为此，Orq 利用以下事实消除了安全连接的二次成本：在实践中，许多实际查询的结构允许我们“动态”连接记录并应用聚合，同时保持结果大小有限。在系统方面，Orq 提供了通用的不经意运算符、数据并行矢量化查询引擎、分摊 MPC 网络成本的通信层以及用于表达关系分析的数据流 API，所有这些都是从头开始构建的。

我们在不同的工作负载上评估 LAN 和 WAN 部署中的 Orq，包括具有多个联接和自定义聚合的复杂查询。与最先进的解决方案相比，Orq 显着减少了 MPC 执行时间，并且可以处理大一个数量级的数据集。对于我们最具挑战性的工作负载，即完整的 TPC-H 基准测试，我们完全在比例因子 10 的 MPC 下报告结果，这一比例以前只能通过信息泄漏或使用可信计算来实现。

items.title: The Design and Implementation of a Virtual Firmware Monitor.
items.abstract:
Low level software is often granted high privilege, yet this need not be the case. Although vendor firmware plays a critical role in the operation and management of the machine, most of its functionality does not require unfettered access to security critical software and data. In this paper we demonstrate that vendor firmware can be safely and efficiently deprivileged, decoupling its functionality from isolation enforcement.

We introduce a new class of systems, called virtual firmware monitors , that run unmodified vendor firmware in userspace through software-based virtualization of the highest privilege mode of the application CPU. We describe the implementation of Miralis, a RISC-V virtual firmware monitor, and develop three security policies to protect the OS, enclaves, and confidential VMs from malicious firmware. We verify key components of Miralis, such as instruction emulation and memory protection, through exhaustive symbolic execution. Finally, we demonstrate that Miralis can effectively virtualize unmodified vendor firmware for two hardware platforms with no performance degradation compared to native execution.
items.abstract_zh:
低级软件通常被授予高权限，但情况并非如此。尽管供应商固件在机器的操作和管理中发挥着关键作用，但其大部分功能并不需要不受限制地访问安全关键软件和数据。在本文中，我们证明可以安全有效地剥夺供应商固件的特权，从而将其功能与隔离执行分离。

我们引入了一类新的系统，称为虚拟固件监视器，它通过应用程序 CPU 最高权限模式的基于软件的虚拟化在用户空间中运行未经修改的供应商固件。我们描述了 Miralis（一种 RISC-V 虚拟固件监视器）的实现，并开发了三种安全策略来保护操作系统、飞地和机密虚拟机免受恶意固件的侵害。我们通过详尽的符号执行来验证 Miralis 的关键组件，例如指令模拟和内存保护。最后，我们证明 Miralis 可以有效地虚拟化两个硬件平台的未修改的供应商固件，与本机执行相比，性能不会下降。

items.title: Atmosphere: Practical Verified Kernels with Rust and Verus.
items.abstract:
Recent advances in programming languages and automated formal reasoning have changed the balance between the complexity and practicality of developing formally verified systems. Our work leverages Verus, a new verifier for Rust that combines ideas of linear types, permissioned reasoning, and automated verification based on satisfiability modulo theories (SMT), for the development of a formally verified microkernel, Atmosphere.

Atmosphere is a full-featured microkernel with support for strict isolation in mixed-criticality systems. We develop all code in Rust and prove its functional correctness, i.e., refinement of a high-level specification, with Verus. Development and verification of 6K lines of executable code required an effort of less than 2.5 person-years (only 1.5 years were spent on verification, another person-year was spent developing non-verified parts of the system). On average, our code has a proof-to-code ratio of 3.32:1 and completes verification in less than 20 seconds on a modern laptop, which we argue is practical for the development of verified systems.
items.abstract_zh:
编程语言和自动形式推理的最新进展改变了开发形式验证系统的复杂性和实用性之间的平衡。我们的工作利用 Verus，一种新的 Rust 验证器，它结合了线性类型、许可推理和基于可满足性模理论 (SMT) 的自动验证的思想，用于开发正式验证的微内核 Atmosphere。

Atmosphere 是一个功能齐全的微内核，支持混合关键性系统中的严格隔离。我们用 Rust 开发所有代码，并使用 Verus 证明其功能正确性，即高级规范的细化。 6K行可执行代码的开发和验证需要不到2.5人年的工作量（仅1.5人年用于验证，另外一个人年用于开发系统的未验证部分）。平均而言，我们的代码的证明与代码比率为 3.32:1，并且在现代笔记本电脑上在不到 20 秒的时间内完成验证，我们认为这对于开发经过验证的系统是实用的。

items.title: Characterizing Mobile SoC for Accelerating Heterogeneous LLM Inference.
items.abstract:
With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technologies such as ChatGPT, AI agents, and video generation, contemporary mobile systems have begun integrating these AI capabilities on local devices to enhance privacy and reduce response latency. To meet the computational demands of AI tasks, current mobile SoCs are equipped with diverse AI accelerators, including GPUs and Neural Processing Units (NPUs). However, there has not been a comprehensive characterization of these heterogeneous processors, and existing designs typically only leverage a single AI accelerator for LLM inference, leading to suboptimal use of computational resources and memory bandwidth.

In this paper, we first summarize key performance characteristics of heterogeneous processors, SoC memory bandwidth, etc. Drawing on these observations, we propose different heterogeneous parallel mechanisms to fully exploit both GPU and NPU computational power and memory bandwidth. We further design a fast synchronization mechanism between heterogeneous processors that leverages the unified memory architecture. By employing these techniques, we present HeteroInfer, the fastest LLM inference engine in mobile devices which supports GPU-NPU heterogeneous execution. Evaluation shows that HeteroInfer delivers a 1.34× to 6.02× end-to-end speedup over state-of-the-art GPU-only and NPU-only LLM engines, while maintaining negligible interference with other applications.
items.abstract_zh:
随着ChatGPT、AI代理和视频生成等人工智能技术的快速发展，当代移动系统已经开始将这些AI功能集成到本地设备上，以增强隐私并减少响应延迟。为了满足AI任务的计算需求，当前的移动SoC配备了多种AI加速器，包括GPU和神经处理单元（NPU）。然而，这些异构处理器尚未得到全面的表征，并且现有设计通常仅利用单个 AI 加速器进行 LLM 推理，导致计算资源和内存带宽的使用不理想。

在本文中，我们首先总结了异构处理器、SoC 内存带宽等关键性能特征。根据这些观察结果，我们提出了不同的异构并行机制，以充分利用 GPU 和 NPU 的计算能力和内存带宽。我们进一步设计了一种利用统一内存架构的异构处理器之间的快速同步机制。通过采用这些技术，我们推出了 HeteroInfer，这是移动设备中最快的 LLM 推理引擎，支持 GPU-NPU 异构执行。评估表明，HeteroInfer 比最先进的仅 GPU 和仅 NPU LLM 引擎提供 1.34 倍至 6.02 倍的端到端加速，同时保持对其他应用程序的干扰可以忽略不计。

items.title: Rearchitecting the Thread Model of In-Memory Key-Value Stores with μTPS.
items.abstract: This paper presents μTPS, a new thread architecture tailored for in-memory key-value stores (KVSs) that operate at tens of millions of operations per second. We show through analysis and demonstration that the widely used run-to-completion thread architecture, which executes monolithic functions from start to finish, often suffers from cache inefficiencies and contention issues. To address this, we revisit the once widely used thread-per-stage (TPS) architecture, but with a fresh perspective – separating cache-resident, contention-free stages and memory-resident, conflict-prone stages into distinct thread pools, and scheduling them with dedicated hardware resources (e.g., CPU cores, cache ways). This novel division enables independent optimization of each stage, significantly improving cache efficiency and mitigating contention. Additionally, μTPS incorporates reconfigurable RPC, resizable caching, and an auto-tuner to enhance its schedulability and performance. We implement two in-memory key-value stores, μTPS-H and μTPS-T, to demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach. Evaluation results show that μTPS achieves higher performance than the run-to-completion counterparts.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 μTPS，这是一种专为内存中键值存储 (KVS) 量身定制的新线程架构，每秒可进行数千万次操作。我们通过分析和演示表明，广泛使用的运行到完成线程架构（从头到尾执行整体函数）经常遭受缓存效率低下和争用问题。为了解决这个问题，我们重新审视曾经广泛使用的每阶段线程 (TPS) 架构，但采用了全新的视角——将驻留在缓存、无争用的阶段和驻留在内存、易发生冲突的阶段分离到不同的线程池中，并使用专用硬件资源（例如 CPU 核心、缓存通道）来调度它们。这种新颖的划分可以实现每个阶段的独立优化，从而显着提高缓存效率并减少争用。此外，μTPS 还结合了可重新配置的 RPC、可调整大小的缓存和自动调节器，以增强其可调度性和性能。我们实现了两个内存中键值存储：μTPS-H 和μTPS-T，以证明这种方法的有效性。评估结果表明，μTPS 比运行到完成的同类产品实现了更高的性能。

items.title: KTransformers: Unleashing the Full Potential of CPU/GPU Hybrid Inference for MoE Models.
items.abstract:
Due to the sparse nature of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, they are particularly suitable for hybrid CPU/GPU inference, especially in low-concurrency scenarios. This hybrid approach leverages both the large, cost-effective memory capacity of CPU/DRAM and the high bandwidth of GPU/VRAM. However, existing hybrid solutions remain bottlenecked by CPU computation limits and CPU-GPU synchronization overheads, severely restricting their ability to efficiently run state-of-the-art large MoE models, such as the 671B DeepSeek-V3/R1.

This paper presents KTransformers, a high-performance inference system designed specifically for efficient heterogeneous computing of diverse MoE models. KTransformers employs optimized, AMX-specialized kernels that fully utilize the computational capabilities of modern CPUs and incorporates an asynchronous CPU-GPU task scheduling mechanism to minimize overhead—achieving 4.62–19.74× prefilling speedups and 1.25–4.09× decoding speedups compared to existing systems.

Furthermore, we propose a novel Expert Deferral mechanism that strategically enhances the potential for overlapping CPU and GPU computations, increasing CPU utilization from typically below 75% to almost 100%. This yields up to 1.45× additional throughput beyond the aforementioned optimizations, with an average model accuracy drop of no more than 0.5% across a diverse set of benchmarks.

The resulting system, KTransformers, substantially enhances the accessibility of large MoE models for local users who prioritize security or intend to dig into the internals of the models. As a result, it has already been widely adopted within both the open-source community and industry.
items.abstract_zh:
由于混合专家（MoE）模型的稀疏性，它们特别适合混合 CPU/GPU 推理，尤其是在低并发场景下。这种混合方法同时利用了 CPU/DRAM 的大容量、经济高效的内存容量和 GPU/VRAM 的高带宽。然而，现有的混合解决方案仍然受到 CPU 计算限制和 CPU-GPU 同步开销的瓶颈，严重限制了它们高效运行最先进的大型 MoE 模型（例如 671B DeepSeek-V3/R1）的能力。

本文介绍了 KTransformers，这是一种专为多种 MoE 模型的高效异构计算而设计的高性能推理系统。 KTransformers 采用优化的 AMX 专用内核，充分利用现代 CPU 的计算能力，并采用异步 CPU-GPU 任务调度机制来最大限度地减少开销，与现有系统相比，实现 4.62–19.74 倍预填充加速和 1.25–4.09 倍解码加速。

此外，我们提出了一种新颖的专家延迟机制，该机制战略性地增强了重叠 CPU 和 GPU 计算的潜力，将 CPU 利用率从通常低于 75% 提高到几乎 100%。与上述优化相比，这可产生高达 1.45 倍的额外吞吐量，在各种基准测试中，平均模型精度下降不超过 0.5%。

由此产生的系统 KTransformers 大大增强了优先考虑安全性或打算深入研究模型内部的本地用户对大型 MoE 模型的可访问性。因此，它已经在开源社区和行业中得到了广泛采用。

items.title: Proto: A Guided Journey through Modern OS Construction.
items.abstract:
Proto is a new instructional OS that runs on commodity, portable hardware. It showcases modern features, including per-app address spaces, threading, commodity filesystems, USB, DMA, multicore support, self-hosted debugging, and a window manager. It supports rich applications such as 2D/3D games, music and video players, and a blockchain miner. Unlike traditional instructional systems, Proto emphasizes engaging, media-rich apps that go beyond basic terminal programs. Our method breaks down a full-featured OS into a set of incremental, self-contained prototypes. Each prototype introduces a minimal set of OS mechanisms, driven by the needs of specific apps. The construction process then progressively enables these apps by bringing up one mechanism at a time.

Proto enables a wider audience to experience building a self-contained software system used in daily life.
items.abstract_zh:
Proto 是一种新的教学操作系统，可在商用便携式硬件上运行。它展示了现代功能，包括每个应用程序地址空间、线程、商品文件系统、USB、DMA、多核支持、自托管调试和窗口管理器。它支持2D/3D游戏、音乐和视频播放器、区块链矿工等丰富的应用程序。与传统的教学系统不同，Proto 强调引人入胜、媒体丰富的应用程序，超越基本的终端程序。我们的方法将功能齐全的操作系统分解为一组增量的、独立的原型。每个原型都引入了一组最小的操作系统机制，由特定应用程序的需求驱动。然后，构建过程通过一次启动一种机制来逐步启用这些应用程序。

Proto 使更广泛的受众能够体验构建日常生活中使用的独立软件系统。

items.title: LithOS: An Operating System for Efficient Machine Learning on GPUs.
items.abstract:
The rapid growth of machine learning (ML) has made GPUs indispensable in datacenters and underscores the urgency of improving their efficiency. However, balancing diverse model demands with high utilization remains a fundamental challenge. Transparent, fine-grained GPU resource management that maximizes utilization, energy efficiency, and isolation requires an OS approach. This paper introduces LithOS , a first step towards a GPU OS.

LithOS includes the following new abstractions and mechanisms for efficient GPU management: (i) a novel TPC Scheduler that supports spatial scheduling at the granularity of individual TPCs, unlocking efficient TPC stealing between workloads; (ii) a transparent kernel atomizer to reduce head-of-line blocking and allow dynamic resource reallocation mid-execution; (iii) a lightweight hardware right-sizing mechanism that dynamically determines the minimal TPC resources needed per atom; and (iv) a transparent power management mechanism that reduces power consumption based upon in-flight work characteristics.

We build LithOS in Rust and evaluate its performance across a broad set of deep learning environments, comparing it to state-of-the-art solutions from NVIDIA and prior research. For inference stacking, LithOS reduces tail latencies by 13× compared to MPS; compared to the best-performing SotA, it reduces tail latencies by 4× while improving aggregate goodput by 1.3×. Furthermore, in hybrid inference-training stacking, LithOS reduces tail latencies by 4.7× compared to MPS; compared to the best-performing SotA, it reduces tail latencies by 1.18× while improving aggregate throughput by 1.35×. Finally, for a modest performance hit under 4%, LithOS's hardware right-sizing provides a quarter of GPU capacity savings on average, while for a 7% hit, LithOS's transparent power management delivers a quarter of GPU total energy savings on average. Overall, LithOS transparently increases GPU efficiency, establishing a foundation for future OS research on GPUs.
items.abstract_zh:
机器学习 (ML) 的快速发展使得 GPU 在数据中心中不可或缺，并凸显了提高其效率的紧迫性。然而，平衡不同模型需求和高利用率仍然是一个根本挑战。透明、细粒度的 GPU 资源管理可以最大限度地提高利用率、能源效率和隔离性，需要采用操作系统方法。本文介绍了 LithOS，这是迈向 GPU 操作系统的第一步。

LithOS 包括以下用于高效 GPU 管理的新抽象和机制：(i) 新颖的 TPC 调度程序，支持单个 TPC 粒度的空间调度，从而解锁工作负载之间的高效 TPC 窃取； (ii) 透明的内核原子化器，以减少队头阻塞并允许在执行过程中进行动态资源重新分配； (iii) 轻量级硬件调整机制，动态确定每个原子所需的最小 TPC 资源； (iv) 透明的电源管理机制，可根据飞行中的工作特性降低功耗。

我们用 Rust 构建 LithOS，并在广泛的深度学习环境中评估其性能，将其与 NVIDIA 和先前研究的最先进的解决方案进行比较。对于推理堆叠，与 MPS 相比，LithOS 将尾部延迟降低了 13 倍；与性能最佳的 SotA 相比，它将尾部延迟降低了 4 倍，同时将总吞吐量提高了 1.3 倍。此外，在混合推理训练堆叠中，与 MPS 相比，LithOS 将尾部延迟降低了 4.7 倍；与性能最佳的 SotA 相比，它将尾部延迟减少了 1.18 倍，同时将总吞吐量提高了 1.35 倍。最后，对于 4% 以下的适度性能影响，LithOS 的硬件调整大小平均可节省四分之一的 GPU 容量，而对于 7% 的性能影响，LithOS 的透明电源管理平均可节省四分之一的 GPU 总能源。总体而言，LithOS 明显提高了 GPU 效率，为未来 GPU 上的操作系统研究奠定了基础。

items.title: Mycroft: Tracing Dependencies in Collective Communication Towards Reliable LLM Training.
items.abstract:
Reliability is essential for ensuring efficiency in LLM training. However, many real-world reliability issues remain difficult to resolve, resulting in wasted resources and degraded model performance. Unfortunately, today's collective communication libraries operate as black boxes, hiding critical information needed for effective root cause analysis.

We propose Mycroft, a lightweight distributed tracing and root cause analysis system designed to address previously hidden reliability issues in collective communication. Mycroft's key idea is to trace collective communication states and leverage internal control and data dependencies to resolve reliability problems in LLM training. Mycroft has been deployed at ByteDance for over six months to debug collective communication-related issues at runtime. It detected anomalies within 15 seconds in 90% of cases and identified the root cause within 20 seconds in 60% of cases. We also conducted extensive fault injection experiments to demonstrate Mycroft's capability and efficiency.
items.abstract_zh:
可靠性对于确保法学硕士培训的效率至关重要。然而，许多现实世界的可靠性问题仍然难以解决，导致资源浪费和模型性能下降。不幸的是，当今的集体通信库就像黑匣子一样运作，隐藏了有效根本原因分析所需的关键信息。

我们提出了 Mycroft，一种轻量级分布式跟踪和根本原因分析系统，旨在解决集体通信中以前隐藏的可靠性问题。 Mycroft 的关键思想是跟踪集体通信状态并利用内部控制和数据依赖性来解决 LLM 培训中的可靠性问题。 Mycroft 已在字节跳动部署了六个多月，用于在运行时调试与通信相关的集体问题。它在 90% 的情况下在 15 秒内检测到异常，并在 60% 的情况下在 20 秒内确定根本原因。我们还进行了广泛的故障注入实验来证明 Mycroft 的能力和效率。

items.title: Tai Chi: A General High-Efficiency Scheduling Framework for SmartNICs in Hyperscale Clouds.
items.abstract:
Cloud service providers increasingly adopt SmartNICs to offload data-plane services (e.g., DPDK and SPDK) and control-plane tasks (such as disk and NIC initialization). Our analysis of production environments reveals that data-plane services statically provision CPUs for peak load, resulting in 67.5% idle CPU cycles during 99% of their runtime in IaaS clouds, leading to wasted CPU resources. On the other hand, control-plane tasks fail to meet critical Service Level Objectives (SLOs), such as virtual machine startup time. Unfortunately, achieving control-plane SLO improvements through co-scheduling with idle data-plane services remains highly challenging, due to the combined effects of intrinsic scheduling latency and the substantial architectural complexity inherent to control-plane ecosystems.

We present Tai Chi, a hardware and software co-designed scheduler that coordinates control-plane tasks and dataplane services through a SmartNIC-accelerated hybrid virtualization. This hybrid framework unifes physical and virtual CPUs within a single OS while providing native inter-process communication semantics among all tasks. By achieving microsecond-scale scheduling precision, it reduces control-plane operation latency by 3.1× (e.g., VM startup) while maintaining data-plane SLO compliance, imposes negligible scheduling overhead, and requires zero code modifications to legacy control-plane systems. Its cross-platform SmartNIC compatibility enables seamless and transparent deployment in the production environment, demonstrating compelling advantages over prior solutions in hyperscale cloud infrastructure.
items.abstract_zh:
云服务提供商越来越多地采用 SmartNIC 来卸载数据平面服务（例如 DPDK 和 SPDK）和控制平面任务（例如磁盘和 NIC 初始化）。我们对生产环境的分析表明，数据平面服务为峰值负载静态配置 CPU，导致 IaaS 云中 99% 的运行时间中有 67.5% 的 CPU 周期空闲，从而导致 CPU 资源浪费。另一方面，控制平面任务无法满足关键的服务级别目标 (SLO)，例如虚拟机启动时间。不幸的是，由于内在调度延迟和控制平面生态系统固有的架构复杂性的综合影响，通过与空闲数据平面服务共同调度来实现控制平面 SLO 改进仍然非常具有挑战性。

我们推出 Tai Chi，这是一种硬件和软件共同设计的调度程序，可通过 SmartNIC 加速的混合虚拟化来协调控制平面任务和数据平面服务。这种混合框架将物理和虚拟 CPU 统一在单个操作系统中，同时在所有任务之间提供本机进程间通信语义。通过实现微秒级的调度精度，它可以将控制平面操作延迟减少 3.1 倍（例如，虚拟机启动），同时保持数据平面 SLO 合规性，调度开销可以忽略不计，并且需要对遗留控制平面系统进行零代码修改。其跨平台 SmartNIC 兼容性可在生产环境中实现无缝、透明的部署，与超大规模云基础设施中的现有解决方案相比，具有显着的优势。

items.title: PrefillOnly: An Inference Engine for Prefill-only Workloads in Large Language Model Applications.
items.abstract: Besides typical generative applications, like ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and Cursor, we observe an emerging trend that LLMs are increasingly used in traditional discriminative tasks, such as recommendation, credit verification, and data labeling. The key characteristic of these emerging use cases is that the LLM generates only a single output token, rather than an arbitrarily long sequence of tokens. We refer to this as a prefill-only workload. However, since existing LLM engines assume arbitrary output lengths, they fail to leverage the unique properties of prefill-only workloads. In this paper, we present PrefillOnly, the first LLM inference engine that improves the inference throughput and latency by fully embracing the properties of prefill-only workloads. First, since it generates only one token, PrefillOnly only needs to store the KV cache of only the last computed layer, rather than of all layers. This drastically reduces the GPU memory footprint of LLM inference and allows handling long inputs without using solutions that reduce throughput, such as cross-GPU KV cache parallelization. Second, because the output length is fixed, rather than arbitrary, PrefillOnly can precisely determine the job completion time (JCT) of each prefill-only request before it starts. This enables efficient JCT-aware scheduling policies such as shortest prefill first. PrefillOnly can process up to 4× larger queries per second without inflating the average and P99 latency.
items.abstract_zh: 除了 ChatGPT、GitHub Copilot 和 Cursor 等典型的生成应用程序之外，我们还观察到一个新兴趋势，即 LLM 越来越多地用于传统的判别性任务，例如推荐、信用验证和数据标记。这些新兴用例的关键特征是 LLM 仅生成单个输出令牌，而不是任意长的令牌序列。我们将此称为仅预填充工作负载。然而，由于现有的 LLM 引擎假定任意输出长度，因此它们无法利用仅预填充工作负载的独特属性。在本文中，我们提出了 PrefillOnly，这是第一个 LLM 推理引擎，它通过完全采用仅预填充工作负载的属性来提高推理吞吐量和延迟。首先，由于它只生成一个令牌，PrefillOnly 只需要存储最后计算层的 KV 缓存，而不是所有层的 KV 缓存。这大大减少了 LLM 推理的 GPU 内存占用，并允许处理长输入，而无需使用降低吞吐量的解决方案，例如跨 GPU KV 缓存并行化。其次，由于输出长度是固定的，而不是任意的，PrefillOnly 可以在每个仅预填充请求开始之前精确确定其作业完成时间（JCT）。这可以实现高效的 JCT 感知调度策略，例如最短预填充优先。 PrefillOnly 每秒最多可以处理 4 倍大的查询，而不会增加平均延迟和 P99 延迟。

items.title: Tiga: Accelerating Geo-Distributed Transactions with Synchronized Clocks.
items.abstract: This paper presents Tiga, a new design for geo-replicated and scalable transactional databases such as Google Spanner. Tiga aims to commit transactions within 1 wide-area roundtrip time, or 1 WRTT, for a wide range of scenarios, while maintaining high throughput with minimal computational overhead. Tiga consolidates concurrency control and consensus, completing both strictly serializable execution and consistent replication in a single round. It uses synchronized clocks to proactively order transactions by assigning each a future timestamp at submission. In most cases, transactions arrive at servers before their future timestamps and are serialized according to the designated timestamp, requiring 1 WRTT to commit. In rare cases, transactions are delayed and proactive ordering fails, in which case Tiga falls back to a slow path, committing in 1.5–2 WRTTs. Compared to state-of-the-art solutions, Tiga can commit more transactions at 1-WRTT latency, and incurs much less throughput overhead. Evaluation results show that Tiga outperforms all baselines, achieving 1.3–7.2× higher throughput and 1.4–4.6× lower latency. Tiga is open-sourced at https://github.com/New-Consensus-Concurrency-Control/Tiga.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 Tiga，这是一种针对地理复制和可扩展事务数据库（例如 Google Spanner）的新设计。 Tiga 的目标是在 1 个广域往返时间或 1 个 WRTT 内提交事务，适用于各种场景，同时以最小的计算开销保持高吞吐量。 Tiga 整合了并发控制和共识，在单轮中完成了严格的可序列化执行和一致复制。它使用同步时钟通过在提交时为每个事务分配一个未来时间戳来主动排序事务。在大多数情况下，事务在未来时间戳之前到达服务器，并根据指定时间戳进行序列化，需要 1 WRTT 才能提交。在极少数情况下，交易会延迟并且主动排序失败，在这种情况下，Tiga 会退回到慢速路径，在 1.5-2 WRTT 内提交。与最先进的解决方案相比，Tiga 可以以 1-WRTT 延迟提交更多事务，并且产生的吞吐量开销要少得多。评估结果表明，Tiga 的性能优于所有基线，吞吐量提高了 1.3–7.2 倍，延迟降低了 1.4–4.6 倍。 Tiga 在 https://github.com/New-Consensus-Concurrency-Control/Tiga 上开源。

items.title: Pie: A Programmable Serving System for Emerging LLM Applications.
items.abstract: Emerging large language model (LLM) applications involve diverse reasoning strategies and agentic workflows, straining the capabilities of existing serving systems built on a monolithic token generation loop. This paper introduces Pie, a programmable LLM serving system designed for flexibility and efficiency. Pie decomposes the traditional generation loop into fine-grained service handlers exposed via an API and delegates control of the generation process to user-provided programs, called inferlets. This enables applications to implement new KV cache strategies, bespoke generation logic, and seamlessly integrate computation and I/O—entirely within the application, without requiring modifications to the serving system. Pie executes inferlets using WebAssembly, benefiting from its lightweight sandboxing. Our evaluation shows Pie matches state-of-the-art performance on standard tasks (3-12% latency overhead) while significantly improving latency and throughput (1.3×-3.4× higher) on agentic workflows by enabling application-specific optimizations.
items.abstract_zh: 新兴的大型语言模型（LLM）应用程序涉及不同的推理策略和代理工作流程，这对构建在整体令牌生成循环上的现有服务系统的能力造成了压力。本文介绍了 Pie，一个专为灵活性和效率而设计的可编程法学硕士服务系统。 Pie 将传统的生成循环分解为通过 API 公开的细粒度服务处理程序，并将生成过程的控制权委托给用户提供的程序（称为 inferlet）。这使得应用程序能够实现新的 KV 缓存策略、定制生成逻辑，并完全在应用程序内无缝集成计算和 I/O，而无需修改服务系统。 Pie 使用 WebAssembly 执行 inferlet，受益于其轻量级沙箱。我们的评估表明，Pie 在标准任务上达到了最先进的性能（3-12% 延迟开销），同时通过启用特定于应用程序的优化，显着改善了代理工作流程的延迟和吞吐量（1.3×-3.4×更高）。

items.title: Mercury: Unlocking Multi-GPU Operator Optimization for LLMs via Remote Memory Scheduling.
items.abstract: In this paper, we propose Mercury, a multi-GPU operator compiler based on a loop-based intermediate representation, CommIR. At the core of Mercury is an abstraction that treats remote GPU memory as an explicitly managed extension of the memory hierarchy, expanding the available storage and communication resources beyond local HBM. This unified view enables the compiler to reason holistically about data placement and inter-device communication, unlocking a vastly larger design space that encompasses and extends beyond existing manual strategies. As a result, Mercury is able to automatically reproduce the performance of hand-optimized baselines like RingAttention and Ulysses, and in some configurations, even discovers more effective strategies that manual designs have overlooked. Our implementation is open-sourced at https://github.com/ChandlerGuan/mercury_artifact.
items.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 Mercury，一种基于循环中间表示 CommIR 的多 GPU 运算符编译器。 Mercury 的核心是一种抽象，它将远程 GPU 内存视为内存层次结构的显式管理扩展，将可用存储和通信资源扩展到本地 HBM 之外。这种统一的视图使编译器能够对数据放置和设备间通信进行整体推理，从而释放出更大的设计空间，涵盖并扩展了现有的手动策略。因此，Mercury 能够自动重现像 RingAttention 和 Ulysses 这样的手动优化基线的性能，并且在某些配置中，甚至发现了手动设计忽略的更有效的策略。我们的实现是开源的，位于 https://github.com/ChandlerGuan/mercury_artifact。

items.title: How to Copy Memory? Coordinated Asynchronous Copy as a First-Class OS Service.
items.abstract: In modern systems, memory copy remains a critical performance bottleneck across various scenarios, playing a pervasive role in system-wide execution such as syscalls, IPC, and user-mode applications. Numerous efforts have aimed at optimizing copy performance, including zero-copy with page remapping and hardware-accelerated copy. However, they typically target specific use cases, such as Linux zero-copy send() for messages of ≥10KB. This paper argues for copy as a first-class OS service, offer ing three key benefits: (1) with the asynchronous copy abstraction provided by the service, applications can overlap their execution with copy; (2) the service can effectively utilize hardware capabilities to enhance copy performance; (3) the service's global view of copies further enables holistic optimization. To this end, we introduce Copier, a new OS service of coordinated asynchronous copy , to serve both user-mode applications and OS services. We build Copier-Linux to demonstrate Copier's ability to improve performance for diverse use cases, including Redis, Protobuf, network stack, proxy, etc. Evaluations show that Copier achieves up to a 1.8 × speedup for real-world applications like Redis and a 1.6 × improvement over zIO, the state-of-the-art in optimizing copy efficiency. To further facilitate adoption, we develop a toolchain to ease the use of Copier. We also integrate Copier into a commercial smartphone OS (HarmonyOS 5.0), achieving promising results.
items.abstract_zh: 在现代系统中，内存复制仍然是各种场景中的关键性能瓶颈，在系统调用、IPC 和用户模式应用程序等系统范围执行中发挥着普遍作用。许多努力旨在优化复制性能，包括带有页面重新映射的零复制和硬件加速复制。然而，它们通常针对特定的用例，例如用于 ≥10KB 消息的 Linux 零拷贝 send()。本文主张将复制作为一流的操作系统服务，提供三个关键好处：（1）通过服务提供的异步复制抽象，应用程序可以将其执行与复制重叠； (2) 该服务能够有效利用硬件能力来增强复制性能； (3)服务的全局副本视图进一步实现了整体优化。为此，我们引入了Copier，一种新的协调异步复制的操作系统服务，为用户模式应用程序和操作系统服务提供服务。我们构建 Copier-Linux 是为了展示 Copier 提高各种用例性能的能力，包括 Redis、Protobuf、网络堆栈、代理等。评估表明，Copier 对 Redis 等实际应用程序实现了高达 1.8 倍的加速，比 zIO 提高了 1.6 倍，zIO 是优化复制效率的最先进技术。为了进一步促进采用，我们开发了一个工具链来简化 Copier 的使用。我们还将Copier集成到商业智能手机操作系统（HarmonyOS 5.0）中，取得了可喜的成果。

items.title: Demeter: A Scalable and Elastic Tiered Memory Solution for Virtualized Cloud via Guest Delegation.
items.abstract: Memory scalability has emerged as a critical bottleneck in virtualized cloud environments. Tiered memory architectures that combine limited fast memory with abundant slower memory offer a promising solution, but existing hypervisor-based approaches suffer from significant performance penalties. We present Demeter, introducing a paradigm shift through guest-delegated tiered memory management based on two key insights: (1) delegation to guests eliminates both expensive access tracking at the hypervisor level and frequent TLB flushes that severely degrade memory virtualization performance under two-dimensional address translation, and (2) Processor Event-Based Sampling, which cannot be effectively utilized by hypervisor-based solutions, remains fully functional and highly efficient when properly leveraged within the guest. Building on these insights, Demeter designs an efficient range-based tiered memory management scheme in guest virtual address space to preserve locality information and employs a double balloon-based provisioning mechanism that maintains cloud elasticity while enabling vendor-specific QoS control. Our evaluation with seven real-world workloads across DRAM+PMEM and DRAM+CXL.mem configurations demonstrates that Demeter improves performance by up to 2× compared to existing hypervisor-based approaches and by 28% on average compared to the next best guest-based alternative. Our implementation is fully open source and publicly available at Zenodo.
items.abstract_zh: 内存可扩展性已成为虚拟化云环境中的关键瓶颈。将有限的快速内存与丰富的慢速内存相结合的分层内存架构提供了一种有前途的解决方案，但现有的基于管理程序的方法遭受了严重的性能损失。我们提出 Demeter，通过基于两个关键见解的来宾委托分层内存管理引入了范式转变：(1) 对来宾的委派消除了虚拟机管理程序级别上昂贵的访问跟踪和频繁的 TLB 刷新，这会严重降低二维地址转换下的内存虚拟化性能；(2) 基于处理器事件的采样无法被基于虚拟机管理程序的解决方案有效利用，但在来宾中正确利用时，仍能保持完整的功能和高效。基于这些见解，Demeter 在来宾虚拟地址空间中设计了一种高效的基于范围的分层内存管理方案，以保留位置信息，并采用基于双气球的配置机制，在保持云弹性的同时实现特定于供应商的 QoS 控制。我们对 DRAM+PMEM 和 DRAM+CXL.mem 配置中的七个实际工作负载进行的评估表明，与现有的基于虚拟机管理程序的方法相比，Demeter 的性能提高了高达 2 倍，与下一个最佳的基于客户的替代方案相比，性能平均提高了 28%。我们的实现是完全开源的，并可在 Zenodo 上公开获取。

items.title: HedraRAG: Co-Optimizing Generation and Retrieval for Heterogeneous RAG Workflows.
items.abstract: In this paper, we identify and tackle emerging system-level challenges in serving heterogeneous RAG workflows, characterized by complex stages and diverse request patterns. We present HedraRAG, a new system built on RAGraph, a graph-based abstraction that exposes optimization opportunities across stage-level parallelism, intra-request similarity, and inter-request skewness. These opportunities are expressed through graph transformations, including node splitting, reordering, edge addition and rewiring. Transformations are dynamically applied to wavefronts of subgraphs across concurrent requests and scheduled onto the CPU-GPU pipeline. Experiments across a wide range of workflows demonstrate that HedraRAG achieves more that 1.5× and up to 5× speedup over existing frameworks, offering a comprehensive solution for heterogeneous RAG workload serving.
items.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们识别并解决了服务异构 RAG 工作流程时出现的新系统级挑战，其特点是复杂的阶段和多样化的请求模式。我们推出了 HedraRAG，这是一个基于 RAGraph 构建的新系统，RAGraph 是一种基于图形的抽象，可提供跨阶段级并行性、请求内相似性和请求间偏度的优化机会。这些机会通过图转换来表达，包括节点分裂、重新排序、边添加和重新连线。转换会跨并发请求动态应用于子图的波前，并调度到 CPU-GPU 管道上。跨各种工作流程的实验表明，HedraRAG 比现有框架实现了 1.5 倍以上甚至高达 5 倍的加速，为异构 RAG 工作负载服务提供了全面的解决方案。

items.title: Mitigating Application Resource Overload with Targeted Task Cancellation.
items.abstract:
Modern software inevitably encounters periods of resource overload, during which it must still sustain high servicelevel objective (SLO) attainment while minimizing request loss. However, achieving this balance is challenging due to subtle and unpredictable internal resource contention among concurrently executing requests. Traditional overload control mechanisms, which rely on global signals, such as queuing delays, fail to handle application resource overload effectively because they cannot accurately predict which requests will monopolize critical resources.

In this paper, we propose Atropos, an overload control framework that proactively cancels the culprit request that cause severe resource contention rather than the victim requests that are blocked by it. Atropos continuously monitors the resource usage of executing requests, identifies the requests contributing most significantly to resource overload, and selectively cancels them. We integrate Atropos into six large-scale applications and evaluate it against 16 real-world overload scenarios. Our results show that Atropos maintains the performance goals while achieving minimal request drop, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art solutions.
items.abstract_zh:
现代软件不可避免地会遇到资源过载的情况，在此期间，它仍然必须维持高服务级别目标 (SLO) 的实现，同时最大限度地减少请求损失。然而，由于并发执行的请求之间存在微妙且不可预测的内部资源争用，因此实现这种平衡具有挑战性。传统的过载控制机制依赖于全局信号（例如排队延迟），无法有效地处理应用程序资源过载，因为它们无法准确预测哪些请求将独占关键资源。

在本文中，我们提出了 Atropos，一种过载控制框架，它主动取消导致严重资源争用的罪魁祸首请求，而不是被它阻止的受害者请求。 Atropos 持续监控执行请求的资源使用情况，识别对资源过载影响最大的请求，并有选择地取消它们。我们将 Atropos 集成到 6 个大型应用程序中，并根据 16 个现实世界的过载场景对其进行评估。我们的结果表明，Atropos 保持了性能目标，同时实现了最小的请求下降，显着优于最先进的解决方案。

items.title: WASIT: Deep and Continuous Differential Testing of WebAssembly System Interface Implementations.
items.abstract: This paper presents WASIT, a powerful specification-driven differential testing framework for WebAssembly (Wasm) system interface (WASI) implementations. WASIT invents several innovative techniques to address the challenges facing state-of-the-art testing approaches when applied to WASI implementations. Specifically, it introduces real-time resource abstraction and tracking to facilitate the generation of meaningful and dependent WASI function calls. It also creates a domain-specific language to automatically filter out uninteresting WASI function argument values by augmenting the WASI specification. Finally, it adopts a decoupled system architecture to achieve smooth co-evolution with WASI. Our evaluation shows that WASIT successfully found 48 new WASI-specific bugs in six popular Wasm runtimes, with 41 confirmed, 37 fixed, and three CVEs assigned.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 WASIT，这是一个强大的规范驱动的差异测试框架，用于 WebAssembly (Wasm) 系统接口 (WASI) 实现。 WASIT 发明了多种创新技术来解决最先进的测试方法在应用于 WASI 实施时所面临的挑战。具体来说，它引入了实时资源抽象和跟踪，以方便生成有意义且相关的 WASI 函数调用。它还创建了一种特定于领域的语言，通过增强 WASI 规范来自动过滤掉不感兴趣的 WASI 函数参数值。最后，采用解耦的系统架构，实现与WASI的平滑协同进化。我们的评估显示，WASIT 在 6 个流行的 Wasm 运行时中成功发现了 48 个新的 WASI 特定错误，其中 41 个已确认，37 个已修复，并分配了 3 个 CVE。

items.title: Analyzing and Enhancing ArckFS: An Anecdotal Example of Benefits of Artifact Evaluation.
items.abstract:
We analyze and enhance Trio and ArckFS by Zhou et al. (SOSP 2023), high-performance NVM file system architecture and file system. A group of authors from KAIST initiated this study through a careful review of the paper and the released artifact, seeking to enhance the Trio work. Their analysis identifies (1) insufficient clarity in the paper on the handling of multi-inode operations, and (2) several implementation bugs in ArckFS that cause occasional operation failures or potential crash inconsistencies during inode creation.

Through close collaboration between the KAIST and Trio authors, we have enhanced the Trio work. (1) We clarify a few relevant rules for ArckFS to handle multi-inode operations. (2) We develop patches for the identified bugs, which essentially maintain the performance claims made in the Trio paper. Our study highlights the crucial role of artifact evaluation in systems research and its potential benefits.
items.abstract_zh:
我们分析并增强了 Zhou 等人的 Trio 和 AckFS。 (SOSP 2023)，高性能NVM文件系统架构和文件系统。 KAIST 的一组作者通过仔细审查论文和已发布的工件发起了这项研究，旨在增强 Trio 的工作。他们的分析发现：(1) 论文中关于处理多 inode 操作的清晰度不够，以及 (2) ArckFS 中的几个实现错误导致 inode 创建过程中偶尔出现操作失败或潜在的崩溃不一致。

通过 KAIST 和 Trio 作者之间的密切合作，我们增强了 Trio 的工作。 (1)我们澄清了AckFS处理多inode操作的一些相关规则。 (2) 我们针对已识别的错误开发补丁，这基本上维持了 Trio 论文中提出的性能声明。我们的研究强调了工件评估在系统研究中的关键作用及其潜在好处。

items.title: Optimistic Recovery for High-Availability Software via Partial Process State Preservation.
items.abstract:
Achieving high availability for modern software requires fast and correct recovery from inevitable faults. This is notoriously difficult. Existing techniques either guarantee correctness by discarding all state but suffer from long downtime, or preserve all state to recover quickly but reintroduce the fault.

We present Phoenix, a framework that enables a new design point of optimistic custom recovery for high-availability software through partial process state preservation. Phoenixmode recovery allows an application to selectively preserve long-lived state, discard transient state, and reset the execution. In the common cases, it combines the effectiveness of full restart with the speed of state reuse. Phoenix offers simple APIs for annotation, supports consistency checks via unsafe region detection, and provides cross-checking validation with default recovery paths for strong correctness. We implement Phoenix in Linux kernel and apply it on six large server applications. Our extensive evaluation of real bugs and fault injection testing shows that Phoenix recovery significantly improves availability while not sacrificing correctness.
items.abstract_zh:
实现现代软件的高可用性需要从不可避免的故障中快速、正确地恢复。这是众所周知的困难。现有技术要么通过丢弃所有状态来保证正确性，但会遭受较长的停机时间，要么保留所有状态以快速恢复，但会重新引入故障。

我们提出了 Phoenix，一个框架，它通过部分进程状态保存实现了高可用性软件的乐观自定义恢复的新设计点。 Phoenixmode 恢复允许应用程序有选择地保留长期状态、丢弃瞬态并重置执行。在常见情况下，它结合了完全重启的有效性和状态重用的速度。 Phoenix 提供简单的注释 API，支持通过不安全区域检测进行一致性检查，并提供与默认恢复路径的交叉检查验证以实现强大的正确性。我们在Linux内核中实现了Phoenix，并将其应用于六个大型服务器应用程序。我们对真实错误和故障注入测试的广泛评估表明，Phoenix 恢复显着提高了可用性，同时又不牺牲正确性。

items.title: Running Consistent Applications Closer to Users with Radical for Lower Latency.
items.abstract: Running applications close to users—in nearby datacenters, at edge points of presence, or in on-premises clusters—is attractive, as it reduces end-to-end latency. Moving strong consistent applications closer to users is difficult, as they incur high latencies either when accessing, or coordinating, their storage system. This restricts such applications to running co-located with their data, in a datacenter. Radical allows these applications to leverage the latency benefits that come from running near users. Radical uses its new LVI protocol to perform all necessary coordination in a single request. This request guarantees linearizability with a combination of locks, a validation step, and write intents. Radical hides the latency of the LVI request by overlapping it with speculative execution of the application. Our evaluation shows that Radical achieves 84–89% of the latency improvement obtainable by moving out of the datacenter, while providing Linearizability.
items.abstract_zh: 在靠近用户的位置（在附近的数据中心、边缘点或本地集群中）运行应用程序很有吸引力，因为它可以减少端到端延迟。将强一致的应用程序移至更靠近用户的位置是很困难的，因为它们在访问或协调其存储系统时会产生高延迟。这限制了此类应用程序与其数据一起在数据中心运行。 Radical 允许这些应用程序利用在用户附近运行所带来的延迟优势。 Radical 使用其新的 LVI 协议在单个请求中执行所有必要的协调。此请求通过锁、验证步骤和写入意图的组合来保证线性化。 Radical 通过将 LVI 请求与应用程序的推测执行重叠来隐藏 LVI 请求的延迟。我们的评估表明，Radical 实现了通过移出数据中心可获得的 84-89% 的延迟改善，同时提供了线性化能力。

items.title: Scalable Address Spaces using Concurrent Interval Skiplist.
items.abstract:
A kernel's address space design can significantly bottleneck multi-threaded applications, as address space operations such as mmap() and munmap() are serialized by coarsegrained locks like Linux's mmap_lock. Such locks have long been known as one of the most intractable contention points in memory management. While prior works have attempted to address this issue, they either fail to sufficiently parallelize operations or are impractical for real-world kernels.

We present the first scalable and practical address space design that parallelizes critical operations. We identify key scalability bottlenecks—many of which extend beyond address spaces—and address them with targeted solutions. At its core is the concurrent interval skiplist , a new data structure that integrates mapping and locking for parallel interval operations. We implement our design on Linux 6.8 and evaluate it on a dual-socket 48-core machine. Our results show a significant throughput improvement of 13.1× for an mmap() microbenchmark, 4.49× for LevelDB, 3.19× for the Apache web server, 1.47× for Metis MapReduce, and 1.27× for Psearchy text indexing.
items.abstract_zh:
内核的地址空间设计可能会严重成为多线程应用程序的瓶颈，因为诸如 mmap() 和 munmap() 之类的地址空间操作是通过粗粒度锁（如 Linux 的 mmap_lock）进行序列化的。长期以来，此类锁一直被认为是内存管理中最棘手的争用点之一。虽然之前的工作已经尝试解决这个问题，但它们要么无法充分并行化操作，要么对于现实世界的内核来说不切实际。

我们提出了第一个可并行化关键操作的可扩展且实用的地址空间设计。我们确定了关键的可扩展性瓶颈（其中许多瓶颈超出了地址空间），并通过有针对性的解决方案来解决它们。其核心是并发间隔跳表，这是一种集成了并行间隔操作的映射和锁定的新数据结构。我们在 Linux 6.8 上实现了我们的设计，并在双路 48 核机器上对其进行了评估。我们的结果显示，mmap() 微基准的吞吐量显着提高了 13.1 倍，LevelDB 提高了 4.49 倍，Apache Web 服务器提高了 3.19 倍，Metis MapReduce 提高了 1.47 倍，Psearchy 文本索引提高了 1.27 倍。

items.title: μFork: Supporting POSIX fork Within a Single-Address-Space OS.
items.abstract:
Single-address-space operating systems have well-known lightweightness benefits that result from their central design idea: the kernel and applications share a unique address space. This model makes these operating systems (OSes) incompatible by design with a large class of software: multiprocess POSIX applications. Indeed, the semantics of the primitive used to create POSIX processes, fork, are inextricably tied to the existence of multiple address spaces.

Prior approaches addressing this issue trade off lightweightness, compatibility and/or isolation. We propose μFork , a single-address-space operating system design supporting POSIX fork on modern hardware without compromising on any of these key objectives. μFork emulates POSIX processes (μprocesses) and achieves fork by creating for the child a copy of the parent μprocess' memory at a different location within a single address space. This approach presents two challenges: relocating the child's absolute memory references (pointers), as well as providing user/kernel and μprocesses isolation without impacting lightweightness. We address them using CHERI. We implement μFork and evaluate it upon three real-world use-cases: Redis snapshots, Nginx multi-worker deployments, and Zygote FaaS worker warm-up. μFork outperforms previous work and traditional monolithic OSes on key lightweightness metrics by an order of magnitude, e.g. it can offer a fork-bound FaaS function throughput 24% higher than that of a monolithic OS, and can fork a μprocess in 54 μs, 3.7× faster than a traditional fork.
items.abstract_zh:
单地址空间操作系统具有众所周知的轻量级优势，这源于其核心设计思想：内核和应用程序共享唯一的地址空间。此模型使这些操作系统 (OS) 在设计上与一大类软件不兼容：多进程 POSIX 应用程序。事实上，用于创建 POSIX 进程的原语（fork）的语义与多个地址空间的存在密不可分。

解决这个问题的现有方法需要权衡轻量性、兼容性和/或隔离性。我们提出 μFork，这是一种单地址空间操作系统设计，支持现代硬件上的 POSIX 分叉，而不会影响任何这些关键目标。 μFork 模拟 POSIX 进程 (μprocesses)，并通过在单个地址空间内的不同位置为子进程创建父 μprocess 内存的副本来实现分叉。这种方法提出了两个挑战：重新定位子进程的绝对内存引用（指针），以及在不影响轻量性的情况下提供用户/内核和微进程隔离。我们使用 CHERI 来解决这些问题。我们实现 μFork 并根据三个实际用例对其进行评估：Redis 快照、Nginx 多工作部署和 Zygote FaaS 工作预热。 μFork 在关键轻量级指标上的表现优于之前的工作和传统的单片操作系统一个数量级，例如它可以提供比单片操作系统高 24% 的分叉绑定 FaaS 函数吞吐量，并且可以在 54 μs 内分叉一个 μprocess，比传统分叉快 3.7 倍。

items.title: Unlocking True Elasticity for the Cloud-Native Era with Dandelion.
items.abstract:
Elasticity is fundamental to cloud computing. An elastic platform can quickly allocate resources to match the demand of each workload as it arrives, rather than pre-provisioning resources to meet performance objectives. However, even serverless platforms — which boot sandboxes in 10s to 100s of milliseconds — are not sufficiently elastic to avoid pre-provisioning expensive resources. Today's FaaS platforms provision many extra, idle sandboxes in memory to reduce the occurrence of slow, cold starts. Initializing securely isolated sandboxes with a POSIX-like computing environment that today's cloud users expect is slow as it requires booting a guest OS and configuring networking.

Our key insight is that the rise of cloud-native application development provides an opportunity to rethink the application interface to the cloud and co-design a much more efficient, elastic computing platform under the hood. We propose Dandelion, an elastic cloud platform with a declarative cloud-native programming model that replaces POSIX-based network interfaces with higher-level (e.g., HTTP-based) interfaces for applications to interact with remote services like cloud storage, databases, and AI inference services. Dandelion executes applications expressed as DAGs of pure compute functions and communication functions.

This enables Dandelion to securely execute compute functions in lightweight sandboxes that cold start in 100s of microseconds , since pure functions do not rely on software environments such as a guest OS. Dandelion makes it practical to boot sandboxes on-demand per request, decreasing performance variability by two to three orders of magnitude compared to Firecracker and reducing committed memory by 96% on average when running the Azure Functions trace.
items.abstract_zh:
弹性是云计算的基础。弹性平台可以快速分配资源，以满足每个工作负载到达时的需求，而不是预先配置资源来满足性能目标。然而，即使是在数十到数百毫秒内启动沙箱的无服务器平台也没有足够的弹性来避免预先配置昂贵的资源。如今的 FaaS 平台在内存中提供了许多额外的空闲沙箱，以减少缓慢、冷启动的发生。使用当今云用户期望的类似 POSIX 的计算环境初始化安全隔离的沙箱速度很慢，因为它需要启动来宾操作系统并配置网络。

我们的主要见解是，云原生应用程序开发的兴起提供了一个重新思考云应用程序接口并共同设计一个更高效、更有弹性的计算平台的机会。我们提出了 Dandelion，这是一个具有声明性云原生编程模型的弹性云平台，用更高级别（例如基于 HTTP）的接口取代基于 POSIX 的网络接口，以便应用程序与云存储、数据库和 AI 推理服务等远程服务进行交互。 Dandelion 执行以纯计算功能和通信功能的 DAG 表示的应用程序。

这使得 Dandelion 能够在数十微秒内冷启动的轻量级沙箱中安全地执行计算函数，因为纯函数不依赖于客户操作系统等软件环境。 Dandelion 可以根据请求按需启动沙箱，与 Firecracker 相比，性能波动性降低了两到三个数量级，并且在运行 Azure Functions 跟踪时平均减少了 96% 的提交内存。

items.title: FlexGuard: Fast Mutual Exclusion Independent of Subscription.
items.abstract: Performance-oriented applications require efficient locks to harness the computing power of multicore architectures. While fast, spinlock algorithms suffer severe performance degradation when thread counts exceed available hardware capacity, i.e. , in oversubscribed scenarios. Existing solutions rely on imprecise heuristics for blocking, leading to suboptimal performance. We present FlexGuard, the first approach that systematically switches from busy-waiting to blocking precisely when a lock-holding thread is preempted. Flex-Guard achieves this by communicating with the OS scheduler via eBPF, unlike prior approaches. FlexGuard matches or improves performance in LevelDB, a memory-optimized database index, PARSEC's Dedup, and SPLASH2X's Raytrace and Streamcluster, boosting throughput by 1–6× in non-oversubscribed and up to 5× in oversubscribed scenarios.
items.abstract_zh: 面向性能的应用程序需要高效的锁来利用多核架构的计算能力。虽然速度很快，但当线程数超过可用硬件容量时（即在超额订阅的情况下），自旋锁算法的性能会严重下降。现有的解决方案依赖于不精确的启发式阻塞，导致性能不佳。我们提出了 FlexGuard，这是第一种在持有锁的线程被抢占时系统地从忙等待切换到阻塞的方法。与之前的方法不同，Flex-Guard 通过 eBPF 与操作系统调度程序通信来实现这一点。 FlexGuard 与 LevelDB、内存优化数据库索引、PARSEC 的 Dedup 以及 SPLASH2X 的 Raytrace 和 Streamcluster 的性能相匹配或提高，在非超额订阅情况下将吞吐量提高 1-6 倍，在超额订阅情况下最高可提高 5 倍。

items.title: Spirit: Fair Allocation of Interdependent Resources in Remote Memory Systems.
items.abstract:
We address the problem of fair resource allocation in multiuser remote memory systems. Allocating local memory (used as cache ) and network bandwidth to remote memory in such systems is challenging due to the complex interdependence between the two resources and application performance. A larger cache may reduce the need for fetching data over the network, while a larger bandwidth may permit more concurrent network requests, avoiding the need for large caches. As a result, applications can achieve the same data access throughput for a wide range of cache and bandwidth allocations. Such interdependence is unique to each application and hard to capture offline.

We propose Spirit, a multi-user framework for fair resource allocation in remote memory systems. Spirit employs a novel Symbiosis algorithm rooted in microeconomic theory that takes application-specific dependency between cache and network bandwidth into account and 'trades' cache and bandwidth resources across users at runtime. We show, both theoretically and empirically, that Symbiosis allocations across users achieve strong fairness properties. Additionally, compared to traditional resource allocation schemes, Spirit improves performance by up to 21.6% across tens of real-world applications with diverse resource needs.
items.abstract_zh:
我们解决多用户远程存储系统中公平资源分配的问题。由于两种资源和应用程序性能之间复杂的相互依赖关系，在此类系统中将本地内存（用作缓存）和网络带宽分配给远程内存具有挑战性。较大的缓存可以减少通过网络获取数据的需要，而较大的带宽可以允许更多并发网络请求，从而避免对大型缓存的需要。因此，应用程序可以在各种缓存和带宽分配下实现相同的数据访问吞吐量。这种相互依赖性对于每个应用程序来说都是独特的，并且很难离线捕获。

我们提出了 Spirit，一个用于远程内存系统中公平资源分配的多用户框架。 Spirit 采用了一种植根于微观经济理论的新颖共生算法，该算法考虑了缓存和网络带宽之间特定于应用程序的依赖性，并在运行时在用户之间“交换”缓存和带宽资源。我们从理论上和经验上都表明，跨用户的共生分配实现了很强的公平性。此外，与传统的资源分配方案相比，Spirit 在数十个具有不同资源需求的实际应用程序中将性能提高了高达 21.6%。

items.title: Mantle: Efficient Hierarchical Metadata Management for Cloud Object Storage Services.
items.abstract:
Cloud Object Storage Services (COSSs) are the primary storage backend in the cloud, supporting large-scale analytics and ML workloads that frequently access deep object paths and update metadata concurrently. However, current COSS architectures incur costly multi-round lookups and high directory contention, delaying job execution. Prior optimizations, largely designed for distributed file systems (with least adoption in clouds), do not apply due to COSS-specific constraints like stateless proxies and limited APIs.

Mantle is a new COSS metadata service for modern cloud workloads. It adopts a two-layer architecture: a scalable, sharded database (TafDB) shared across namespaces and a per-namespace, single-server IndexNode consolidating lightweight directory metadata. With a fine-grained division of metadata and responsibility, Mantle supports up to 10 billion objects or directories in a single namespace and achieves 1.8 million lookups per second through scalable execution of single-RPC lookups on IndexNode. It also delivers up to 58K directory updates per second under high contention by integrating out-of-place delta updates in TafDB and offloading loop detection for cross-directory renames to IndexNode, both effectively eliminating coordination bottlenecks. Compared to the metadata services of Tectonic, InfiniFS and LocoFS, Mantle reduces metadata latency by 6.6-99.1% and improves throughput by 0.07-115.00×. With data access enabled, it shortens job completion times by 63.3–93.3% for interactive Spark analytics and 38.5–47.7% for AI-driven audio preprocessing tasks. Mantle has been deployed on Baidu Object Storage (BOS) for over 2 years, a service offered by Baidu Canghai Storage.
items.abstract_zh:
云对象存储服务 (COSS) 是云中的主要存储后端，支持频繁访问深层对象路径并同时更新元数据的大规模分析和 ML 工作负载。然而，当前的 COSS 架构会产生成本高昂的多轮查找和高目录争用，从而延迟作业执行。之前的优化主要是为分布式文件系统设计的（在云中采用最少），但由于 COSS 特定的限制（例如无状态代理和有限的 API）而不再适用。

Mantle 是一项针对现代云工作负载的新 COSS 元数据服务。它采用两层架构：跨命名空间共享的可扩展分片数据库（TafDB）和每个命名空间的单服务器 IndexNode，整合轻量级目录元数据。通过元数据和职责的细粒度划分，Mantle 在单个命名空间中支持多达 100 亿个对象或目录，并通过在 IndexNode 上可扩展地执行单 RPC 查找来实现每秒 180 万次查找。它还通过在 TafDB 中集成异地增量更新以及将跨目录重命名的循环检测卸载到 IndexNode，在高争用情况下每秒提供高达 58K 的目录更新，两者都有效地消除了协调瓶颈。与 Tectonic、InfiniFS 和 LocoFS 的元数据服务相比，Mantle 将元数据延迟降低了 6.6-99.1%，吞吐量提高了 0.07-115.00 倍。启用数据访问后，交互式 Spark 分析的作业完成时间缩短了 63.3–93.3%，AI 驱动的音频预处理任务的作业完成时间缩短了 38.5–47.7%。 Mantle 已在百度对象存储（BOS）上部署超过 2 年，该服务由百度沧海存储提供。

items.title: Orthrus: Efficient and Timely Detection of Silent User Data Corruption in the Cloud with Resource-Adaptive Computation Validation.
items.abstract: Even with substantial endeavors to test and validate processors, computational errors may still arise post-installation. One particular category of CPU errors transpires discreetly, without crashing applications or triggering hardware warnings. These elusive errors pose a significant threat by undermining user data, and their detection is challenging. This paper introduces Orthrus, a solution for the timely detection of silent user data corruption caused by post-installation CPU errors. Orthrus safeguards user data in cloud applications by providing simple annotations and compiler support for users to identify data operators and validating these operators asynchronously across cores while maintaining a low overhead (2%–6%), making it practical for production deployment. Our evaluation, using carefully injected errors, demonstrates that Orthrus can detect 87% of data corruptions with just a single core dedicated to validation, increasing to 91% and 96% when two and four cores are used, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 即使付出了大量努力来测试和验证处理器，安装后仍然可能出现计算错误。一类特定的 CPU 错误会悄悄发生，不会导致应用程序崩溃或触发硬件警告。这些难以捉摸的错误会破坏用户数据，从而构成重大威胁，并且它们的检测具有挑战性。本文介绍了 Orthrus，这是一种及时检测由安装后 CPU 错误导致的无提示用户数据损坏的解决方案。 Orthrus 通过为用户提供简单的注释和编译器支持来识别数据运算符并跨内核异步验证这些运算符，同时保持较低的开销 (2%–6%)，从而保护云应用程序中的用户数据，使其适用于生产部署。我们使用仔细注入的错误进行的评估表明，Orthrus 只需一个专用于验证的核心即可检测到 87% 的数据损坏，当使用两个和四个核心时，分别增加到 91% 和 96%。

items.title: TrainVerify: Equivalence-Based Verification for Distributed LLM Training.
items.abstract:
Training large language models (LLMs) at scale requires parallel execution across thousands of devices, incurring enormous computational costs. Yet, these costly distributed trainings are prone to correctness bugs, causing silent errors and potentially wasting millions of GPU hours. These bugs are challenging to expose through testing.

We introduce TrainVerify, a system for verifiable distributed training of LLMs to eliminate parallelization bugs. Given a deep learning model's logical specification as the ground truth, TrainVerify formally verifies that a distributed parallel execution plan is mathematically equivalent to it. Direct verification is notoriously difficult due to the sheer scale of LLMs which often involves billions of variables and highly intricate computation graphs. Therefore, TrainVerify introduces a stage-wise parallel verification algorithm and shape-reduction techniques that significantly reduce complexity while preserving formal correctness. TrainVerify scales to frontier LLMs, including the successful verification of the Llama3 405B and DeepSeek-V3 671B training plans.
items.abstract_zh:
大规模训练大型语言模型 (LLM) 需要在数千台设备上并行执行，从而产生巨大的计算成本。然而，这些成本高昂的分布式训练很容易出现正确性错误，导致无声错误并可能浪费数百万个 GPU 小时。这些错误很难通过测试暴露出来。

我们引入了 TrainVerify，这是一个用于可验证的 LLM 分布式训练的系统，以消除并行化错误。将深度学习模型的逻辑规范作为基本事实，TrainVerify 正式验证分布式并行执行计划在数学上与其等效。由于法学硕士规模庞大，通常涉及数十亿个变量和高度复杂的计算图，直接验证是出了名的困难。因此，TrainVerify 引入了分阶段并行验证算法和形状缩减技术，可显着降低复杂性，同时保持形式正确性。 TrainVerify 扩展到前沿法学硕士，包括 Llama3 405B 和 DeepSeek-V3 671B 培训计划的成功验证。

items.title: Ghost in the Android Shell: Pragmatic Test-oracle Specification of a Production Hypervisor.
items.abstract:
Developing systems code that robustly provides its intended security guarantees remains very challenging: conventional practice does not suffice, and full functional verification, while now feasible in some contexts, has substantial barriers to entry and use.

In this paper, we explore an alternative, more lightweight approach to building confidence for a production hypervisor: the pKVM hypervisor developed by Google to protect virtual machines and the Android kernel from each other. The basic approach is very simple and dates back to the 1970s: we specify the desired behaviour in a way that can be used as a test oracle, and check correspondence between that and the implementation at runtime. The setting makes that challenging in several ways: the implementation and specification are intertwined with the underlying architecture; the hypervisor is highly concurrent; the specification has to be loose in certain ways; the hypervisor runs bare-metal in a privileged exception level; naive random testing would quickly crash the whole system; and the hypervisor is written in C using conventional methods. We show how all of these can be overcome to make a practically useful specification, finding a number of critical bugs in pKVM along the way.

This is not at all what conventional developers (nor what formal verifiers) normally do – but we argue that, with the appropriate mindset, they easily could and should.
items.abstract_zh:
开发能够可靠地提供其预期安全保证的系统代码仍然非常具有挑战性：传统实践是不够的，而完整的功能验证虽然现在在某些情况下是可行的，但在进入和使用方面存在很大的障碍。

在本文中，我们探索了一种替代的、更轻量级的方法来为生产虚拟机管理程序建立信心：由 Google 开发的 pKVM 虚拟机管理程序，用于保护虚拟机和 Android 内核免受彼此的影响。基本方法非常简单，可以追溯到 20 世纪 70 年代：我们以可用作测试预言机的方式指定所需的行为，并在运行时检查该行为与实现之间的对应关系。这种设置在几个方面使这一点变得具有挑战性：实现和规范与底层架构交织在一起；虚拟机管理程序是高度并发的；规范在某些方面必须宽松；虚拟机管理程序以特权异常级别在裸机上运行；幼稚的随机测试很快就会使整个系统崩溃； Hypervisor采用常规方法用C语言编写。我们展示了如何克服所有这些问题以制定实用的规范，并在此过程中发现 pKVM 中的许多关键错误。

这根本不是传统开发人员（也不是正式验证者）通常所做的事情——但我们认为，只要有适当的心态，他们很容易就能而且应该这样做。

items.title: TRIP: Coercion-resistant Registration for E-Voting with Verifiability and Usability in Votegral.
items.abstract: Online voting is convenient and flexible, but amplifies the risks of voter coercion and vote buying. One promising mitigation strategy enables voters to give a coercer fake voting credentials, which silently cast votes that do not count. Current systems along these lines make problematic assumptions about credential issuance, however, such as strong trust in a registrar and/or in voter-controlled hardware, or expecting voters to interact with multiple registrars. Votegral is the first coercion-resistant voting architecture that leverages the physical security of in-person registration to address these credential-issuance challenges, amortizing the convenience costs of in-person registration by reusing credentials across successive elections. Votegral's registration component, TRIP, gives voters a kiosk in a privacy booth with which to print real and fake credentials on paper, eliminating dependence on trusted hardware in credential issuance. The voter learns and can verify in the privacy booth which credential is real, but real and fake credentials thereafter appear indistinguishable to others. Only voters actually under coercion, a hopefully-rare case, need to trust the kiosk. To achieve verifiability, each paper credential encodes an interactive zero-knowledge proof, which is sound in real credentials but unsound in fake credentials. Voters observe the difference in the order of printing steps, but need not understand the technical details. Experimental results with our prototype suggest that Votegral is practical and sufficiently scalable for real-world elections. User-visible latency of credential issuance in TRIP is at most 19.7 seconds even on resource-constrained kiosk hardware, making it suitable for registration at remote locations or on battery power. A companion usability study indicates that TRIP's usability is competitive with other e-voting systems including some lacking coercion resistance, and formal proofs support TRIP's combination of coercion-resistance and verifiability.
items.abstract_zh: 网络投票方便灵活，但也放大了选民胁迫和买票的风险。一种有希望的缓解策略使选民能够向胁迫者提供虚假的投票凭证，从而默默地投出不计数的选票。然而，当前的系统对证书颁发做出了有问题的假设，例如对注册商和/或选民控制的硬件的强烈信任，或者期望选民与多个注册商进行交互。 Votegral 是第一个抗强制投票架构，它利用亲自注册的物理安全性来解决这些凭证颁发挑战，通过在连续选举中重复使用凭证来分摊亲自注册的便利成本。 Votegral 的注册组件 TRIP 在隐私亭为选民提供了一个信息亭，可以在纸上打印真实和虚假的凭证，从而消除了凭证颁发中对可信硬件的依赖。选民可以在隐私亭中了解并验证哪些凭证是真实的，但此后其他人就无法区分真假凭证。只有真正受到胁迫的选民（希望这种情况很少见）才需要信任这个信息亭。为了实现可验证性，每个纸质凭证都编码了一个交互式零知识证明，该证明在真实凭证中是可靠的，但在虚假凭证中是不可靠的。投票者观察打印步骤顺序的差异，但不需要了解技术细节。我们原型的实验结果表明，Votegral 对于现实世界的选举来说是实用的并且具有足够的可扩展性。即使在资源有限的自助服务终端硬件上，TRIP 中用户可见的凭证颁发延迟也最多为 19.7 秒，这使其适合在远程位置或使用电池进行注册。一项配套的可用性研究表明，TRIP 的可用性与其他电子投票系统（包括一些缺乏抗强制性的系统）相比具有竞争力，并且正式证据支持 TRIP 的抗强制性和可验证性的结合。

items.title: Scalable Far Memory: Balancing Faults and Evictions.
items.abstract:
Page-based far memory systems transparently expand an application's memory capacity beyond a single machine without modifying application code. However, existing systems are tailored to scenarios with low application thread counts, and fail to scale on today's multi-core machines. This makes them unsuitable for data-intensive applications that both rely on far memory support and scale with increasing thread count. Our analysis reveals that this poor scalability stems from inefficient holistic coordination between page fault-in and eviction operations. As thread count increases, current systems encounter scalability bottlenecks in TLB shootdowns, page accounting, and memory allocation.

This paper presents three design principles that address these scalability challenges and enable efficient memory offloading. These principles are always-asynchronous decoupling to handle eviction operations as asynchronously as possible, cross-batch pipelined execution to avoid idle waiting periods, and scalability prioritization to avoid synchronization overheads at high thread counts at the cost of eviction accuracy. We implement these principles in both the Linux kernel and a library OS. Our evaluation shows that this approach increases throughput for batch-processing applications by up to 4.2× and reduces 99 th percentile latency for a latency-critical memcached application by 94.5%.
items.abstract_zh:
基于页面的远内存系统可以透明地将应用程序的内存容量扩展到单台机器之外，而无需修改应用程序代码。然而，现有系统是针对应用程序线程数较低的场景量身定制的，无法在当今的多核机器上进行扩展。这使得它们不适合数据密集型应用程序，这些应用程序既依赖远内存支持，又随着线程数量的增加而扩展。我们的分析表明，这种糟糕的可扩展性源于页面错误和逐出操作之间低效的整体协调。随着线程数量的增加，当前系统在 TLB 关闭、页面统计和内存分配方面遇到了可扩展性瓶颈。

本文提出了三个设计原则，可解决这些可扩展性挑战并实现高效的内存卸载。这些原则是始终异步解耦，以尽可能异步地处理逐出操作，跨批次流水线执行，以避免空闲等待期，以及可扩展性优先级，以避免高线程数下的同步开销，但以逐出精度为代价。我们在 Linux 内核和库操作系统中实现了这些原则。我们的评估表明，这种方法可将批处理应用程序的吞吐量提高高达 4.2 倍，并将延迟关键型 Memcached 应用程序的 99% 延迟降低 94.5%。

items.title: Device-Assisted Live Migration of RDMA Devices.
items.abstract:
Recently, we have seen growing pressure to move highperformance workloads, such as HPC and AI, to cloud environments that offer more affordable and manageable infrastructure. These workloads require direct access to RDMA devices for high-performance communication. Device passthrough, however, violates the decoupling between the guest OS and the underlying hardware, making Live Migration (LM) extremely challenging [29, 38, 40, 42, 48].

This paper presents a method for migrating a collection of directly interacting hardware devices in a manner that is transparent to both the VM and its network peers. We propose a device-assisted solution that includes (a) a generic device-hypervisor interface, (b) the design and implementation of LM support for the NVIDIA ConnectX family of network adapters, and (c) a novel scheme to quiesce direct communication over the memory fabric (e.g., PCIe).

We demonstrate transparent migration of HPC and AI workloads in accelerated virtual environments. Our approach incurs no runtime overhead or performance degradation after migration and achieves sub-second downtimes even for VMs with high RDMA resource utilization.
items.abstract_zh:
最近，我们看到将高性能工作负载（例如 HPC 和 AI）迁移到提供更实惠且易于管理的基础设施的云环境的压力越来越大。这些工作负载需要直接访问 RDMA 设备以实现高性能通信。然而，设备直通违反了来宾操作系统和底层硬件之间的解耦，使得实时迁移（LM）极具挑战性[29,38,40,42,48]。

本文提出了一种以对虚拟机及其网络对等方透明的方式迁移直接交互的硬件设备集合的方法。我们提出了一种设备辅助解决方案，其中包括 (a) 通用设备管理程序接口、(b) NVIDIA ConnectX 系列网络适配器的 LM 支持的设计和实现，以及 (c) 一种通过内存结构（例如 PCIe）停止直接通信的新颖方案。

我们演示了加速虚拟环境中 HPC 和 AI 工作负载的透明迁移。我们的方法在迁移后不会产生运行时开销或性能下降，甚至对于具有高 RDMA 资源利用率的虚拟机也能实现亚秒级停机时间。

items.title: Moirai: Optimizing Placement of Data and Compute in Hybrid Clouds.
items.abstract:
The deployment of large-scale data analytics between on-premise and cloud sites, i.e., hybrid clouds , requires careful partitioning of both data and computation to avoid massive networking costs. We present Moirai, a cost-optimization framework that analyzes job accesses and data dependencies and optimizes the placement of both in hybrid clouds. Moirai informs the job scheduler of data location and access predictions, so it can determine where jobs should be executed to minimize data transfer costs. Our optimizer achieves scalability and cost efficiency by exploiting recurring jobs to identify data dependencies and job access characteristics and reduces the search space by excluding data not accessed recently.

We validate Moirai using 4-month traces that span 66.7M queries accessing 13.3EB from Presto and Spark clusters deployed at Uber, a multi-national transportation company leveraging large-scale data analytics for its operations. Moirai reduces hybrid cloud deployment costs by over 97% relative to the state-of-the-art partitioning approach from Alibaba and other public approaches. The savings come from 95–99.5% reduction in cloud egress, up to 99% reduction in replication, and 89–98% reduction in on-premises network infrastructure requirements. We also describe concrete steps being taken towards deploying Moirai in production.
items.abstract_zh:
在本地和云站点（即混合云）之间部署大规模数据分析需要仔细划分数据和计算，以避免大量的网络成本。我们推出了 Moirai，这是一个成本优化框架，可分析作业访问和数据依赖性并优化两者在混合云中的放置。 Moirai 向作业调度程序通知数据位置和访问预测，以便它可以确定应在何处执行作业以最大限度地降低数据传输成本。我们的优化器通过利用重复作业来识别数据依赖性和作业访问特征来实现可扩展性和成本效率，并通过排除最近未访问的数据来减少搜索空间。

我们使用 4 个月的跟踪来验证 Moirai，这些跟踪跨越 6670 万个查询，从部署在 Uber 的 Presto 和 Spark 集群访问 13.3EB，Uber 是一家利用大规模数据分析进行运营的跨国运输公司。相对于阿里巴巴和其他公共方法最先进的分区方法，Moirai 将混合云部署成本降低了 97% 以上。节省的原因是云出口量减少了 95-99.5%，复制量减少了 99%，以及本地网络基础设施要求减少了 89-98%。我们还描述了在生产中部署 Moirai 所采取的具体步骤。

items.title: Coyote v2: Raising the Level of Abstraction for Data Center FPGAs.
items.abstract: In the trend towards hardware specialization, FPGAs play a dual role as accelerators for offloading, e.g., network virtualization, and as a vehicle for prototyping and exploring hardware designs. While FPGAs offer versatility and performance, integrating them in larger systems remains challenging. Thus, recent efforts have focused on raising the level of abstraction through better interfaces and high-level programming languages. Yet, there is still quite some room for improvement. In this paper, we present Coyote v2, an open-source FPGA shell built with a novel, three-layer hierarchical design supporting dynamic partial reconfiguration of services and user logic, with a unified logic interface, and high-level software abstractions which facilitate application deployment, multi-tenancy and transparent workload pipelining. Experimental results indicate Coyote v2 reduces synthesis times between 15% and 20% and run-time reconfiguration times by an order of magnitude, when compared to existing systems. We also demonstrate the advantages of Coyote v2 by deploying several realistic applications, including HyperLogLog cardinality estimation, AES encryption, and neural network inference.
