Finally, Coyote v2 places a great deal of emphasis on integration with real systems through reusable and reconfigurable services, including a fully RoCE v2-compliant networking stack, a shared virtual memory model with the host, and a DMA engine between FPGAs and GPUs. We demonstrate these features by, e.g., seamlessly deploying an FPGA-accelerated neural network from Python.
items.abstract_zh: 在硬件专业化的趋势中，FPGA 发挥着双重作用：作为卸载（例如网络虚拟化）的加速器，以及作为原型设计和探索硬件设计的工具。虽然 FPGA 提供了多功能性和性能，但将它们集成到更大的系统中仍然具有挑战性。因此，最近的努力集中在通过更好的接口和高级编程语言来提高抽象级别。然而，仍有相当大的改进空间。在本文中，我们介绍了 Coyote v2，这是一种开源 FPGA shell，采用新颖的三层分层设计构建，支持服务和用户逻辑的动态部分重新配置，具有统一的逻辑接口和高级软件抽象，有助于应用程序部署、多租户和透明的工作负载流水线。实验结果表明，与现有系统相比，Coyote v2 将综合时间减少了 15% 至 20%，并将运行时重新配置时间减少了一个数量级。我们还通过部署几个实际应用程序来展示 Coyote v2 的优势，包括 HyperLogLog 基数估计、AES 加密和神经网络推理。最后，Coyote v2 非常重视通过可重用和可重新配置的服务与真实系统集成，包括完全符合 RoCE v2 的网络堆栈、与主机共享的虚拟内存模型以及 FPGA 和 GPU 之间的 DMA 引擎。我们通过从 Python 无缝部署 FPGA 加速神经网络等方式来演示这些功能。

items.title: METIS: Fast Quality-Aware RAG Systems with Configuration Adaptation.
items.abstract: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) allows LLMs (large language models) to generate better responses with external knowledge, but using more external knowledge causes higher response delay. Prior work focuses either on reducing the response delay (e.g. , better scheduling of RAG queries) or on maximizing quality (e.g. , tuning the RAG workflow), but they fall short in systematically balancing the tradeoff between the delay and quality of RAG responses. To balance both quality and response delay, this paper presents METIS, the first RAG system that jointly schedules queries and adapts the key RAG configurations of each query, such as the number of retrieved text chunks and synthesis methods. Using four popular RAG-QA datasets, we show that compared to the state-of-the-art RAG optimization schemes, METIS reduces the generation latency by 1.64 – 2.54× without sacrificing generation quality.
items.abstract_zh: RAG（检索增强生成）允许LLM（大型语言模型）利用外部知识生成更好的响应，但使用更多的外部知识会导致更高的响应延迟。先前的工作要么侧重于减少响应延迟（例如，更好地调度 RAG 查询），要么侧重于最大化质量（例如，调整 RAG 工作流程），但它们未能系统地平衡 RAG 响应的延迟和质量之间的权衡。为了平衡质量和响应延迟，本文提出了 METIS，这是第一个联合调度查询并调整每个查询的关键 RAG 配置（例如检索的文本块的数量和合成方法）的 RAG 系统。使用四个流行的 RAG-QA 数据集，我们表明，与最先进的 RAG 优化方案相比，METIS 将生成延迟降低了 1.64 – 2.54 倍，而无需牺牲生成质量。

items.title: TickTock: Verified Isolation in a Production Embedded OS.
items.abstract: We present a case study formally verifying process isolation in the Tock production microcontroller OS kernel. Tock combines hardware memory protection units and language-level techniques—by writing the kernel in Rust—to enforce isolation between user and kernel code. Our effort to verify Tock's process abstraction unearthed multiple, subtle bugs that broke isolation—many allowing malicious applications to compromise the whole OS. We describe this effort and TickTock, our fork of the Tock operating system kernel that eliminates isolation bugs by construction. TickTock uses Flux, an SMT-based Rust verifier, to formally specify and verify process isolation for all ARMv7-M platforms Tock supports and for three RISC-V 32-bit platforms. Our verification-guided design and implementation led to a new, granular process abstraction that is simpler than Tock's, has formal security guarantees (that are verified in half a minute), and outperforms Tock on certain critical code paths.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个案例研究，正式验证 Tock 生产微控制器操作系统内核中的进程隔离。 Tock 将硬件内存保护单元和语言级技术相结合（通过用 Rust 编写内核）来强制用户和内核代码之间的隔离。我们验证 Tock 进程抽象的努力发现了多个破坏隔离的微妙错误，其中许多错误允许恶意应用程序危害整个操作系统。我们描述了这项工作和 TickTock，我们的 Tock 操作系统内核的分支，通过构造消除了隔离错误。 TickTock 使用 Flux（一种基于 SMT 的 Rust 验证器）来正式指定和验证 Tock 支持的所有 ARMv7-M 平台以及三个 RISC-V 32 位平台的进程隔离。我们以验证为导向的设计和实现带来了一种新的细粒度流程抽象，它比 Tock 更简单，具有正式的安全保证（半分钟内验证），并且在某些关键代码路径上优于 Tock。

items.title: Tock: From Research To Securing 10 Million Computers.
items.abstract: Tock began 10 years ago as a research operating system developed by academics to help other academics build urban sensing applications. By leveraging a new language (Rust) and new hardware protection mechanisms, Tock enabled "Multiprogramming a 64 kB Computer Safely and Efficiently". Today, it is an open-source project with a vibrant community of users and contributors. It is deployed on root-of-trust hardware in data-center servers and on millions of laptops; it is used to develop automotive and space products, wearable electronics, and hardware security tokens—all while remaining a platform for operating systems research. This paper focuses on the impact of Tock's technical design on its adoption, the challenges and unexpected benefits of using a type-safe language (Rust)—particularly in security-sensitive settings—and the experience of supporting a production open-source operating system from academia.
items.abstract_zh: Tock 于 10 年前开始作为一个由学者开发的研究操作系统，旨在帮助其他学者构建城市传感应用程序。通过利用新语言 (Rust) 和新的硬件保护机制，Tock 实现了“安全高效地对 64 kB 计算机进行多道编程”。如今，它是一个开源项目，拥有充满活力的用户和贡献者社区。它部署在数据中心服务器的信任根硬件和数百万台笔记本电脑上；它用于开发汽车和航天产品、可穿戴电子产品和硬件安全令牌，同时仍然是操作系统研究的平台。本文重点介绍了 Tock 的技术设计对其采用的影响、使用类型安全语言 (Rust) 的挑战和意想不到的好处（特别是在安全敏感的设置中）以及学术界支持生产开源操作系统的经验。

items.title: Tempo: Compiled Dynamic Deep Learning with Symbolic Dependence Graphs.
items.abstract:
Deep learning (DL) algorithms are often defined in terms of temporal relationships : a tensor at one timestep may depend on tensors from earlier or later timesteps. Such dynamic dependencies (and corresponding dynamic tensor shapes) are difficult to express and optimize: while eager DL systems support such dynamism, they cannot apply compiler-based optimizations; graph-based systems require static tensor shapes, which forces users to pad tensors or break-up programs into multiple static graphs.

We describe Tempo, a new DL system that combines the dynamism of eager execution with the whole-program optimizations of graph-based compilation. Tempo achieves this through a declarative programming model with recurrent tensors , which include explicit temporal dimensions. Temporal dimensions can be indexed using symbolic expressions to express dynamic dependencies on past and future tensors. Based on this, Tempo constructs a symbolic dependence graph , which concisely encodes dynamic dependencies between operators, and applies whole-program optimizations, such as algebraic simplifications, vectorization, tiling, and fusion. By tiling dynamic dependencies into static-size blocks, Tempo can also reuse existing static code-generators. It then uses a polyhedral model to find a feasible execution schedule, which includes memory management operations. We show that Tempo achieves a 7× speedup over JAX for Llama-3.2-3B decoding; for reinforcement learning algorithms, Tempo achieves a 54× speedup, with 16× lower peak memory usage.
items.abstract_zh:
深度学习 (DL) 算法通常根据时间关系来定义：一个时间步长的张量可能取决于较早或较晚时间步长的张量。这种动态依赖关系（以及相应的动态张量形状）很难表达和优化：虽然急切的深度学习系统支持这种动态性，但它们无法应用基于编译器的优化；基于图的系统需要静态张量形状，这迫使用户将张量或分解程序填充到多个静态图中。

我们描述了 Tempo，这是一种新的深度学习系统，它将急切执行的活力与基于图的编译的整个程序优化相结合。 Tempo 通过具有循环张量的声明性编程模型来实现这一点，其中包括显式的时间维度。可以使用符号表达式对时间维度进行索引，以表达对过去和未来张量的动态依赖关系。在此基础上，Tempo 构建了一个符号依赖图，它简洁地编码了算子之间的动态依赖关系，并应用了整个程序的优化，例如代数简化、矢量化、平铺和融合。通过将动态依赖项平铺到静态大小的块中，Tempo 还可以重用现有的静态代码生成器。然后，它使用多面体模型来找到可行的执行计划，其中包括内存管理操作。我们证明 Tempo 在 Llama-3.2-3B 解码方面比 JAX 实现了 7 倍的加速；对于强化学习算法，Tempo 实现了 54 倍的加速，同时峰值内存使用量降低了 16 倍。

items.title: Loom: Efficient Capture and Querying of High-Frequency Telemetry.
items.abstract:
To debug performance issues, engineers often rely on high-frequency telemetry (HFT) from sources like perf, DTrace, or eBPF, which can generate millions of records per second. Current database systems are too slow to capture such highrate data in its entirety, and the de facto standard approach of writing HFT to raw files makes queries slow and cumbersome. Engineers must therefore either work with incomplete data, which risks missing critical events, or accept slow queries.

Loom is a new system specialized for capturing and analyzing HFT with timely, interactive queries. Key to Loom's design is that it combines the high ingest capability of log-based storage with lightweight, sparse, and domain-specific indexes that accelerate queries. This design strikes a balance: it prioritizes capturing complete data at high rate while indexing just enough to support interactive queries on HFT.

Experiments show that Loom supports both higher ingest throughput and lower query latency than best-in-class systems for ingest-optimized storage (FishStore) and time series databases (InfluxDB), all while consuming substantially fewer host resources and ensuring data completeness.
items.abstract_zh:
为了调试性能问题，工程师通常依赖来自 perf、DTrace 或 eBPF 等来源的高频遥测 (HFT)，这些数据每秒可以生成数百万条记录。当前的数据库系统速度太慢，无法完整捕获如此高速率的数据，而将 HFT 写入原始文件的事实上的标准方法使查询变得缓慢而麻烦。因此，工程师必须要么处理不完整的数据，这可能会丢失关键事件，要么接受缓慢的查询。

Loom 是一个专门用于通过及时的交互式查询捕获和分析高频交易的新系统。 Loom 设计的关键在于，它将基于日志的存储的高摄取能力与可加速查询的轻量级、稀疏且特定于域的索引相结合。这种设计达到了平衡：它优先考虑高速捕获完整数据，同时索引足以支持高频交易的交互式查询。

实验表明，与用于摄取优化存储 (FishStore) 和时间序列数据库 (InfluxDB) 的同类最佳系统相比，Loom 支持更高的摄取吞吐量和更低的查询延迟，同时消耗的主机资源大幅减少并确保数据完整性。

items.title: Sailor: Automating Distributed Training over Dynamic, Heterogeneous, and Geo-distributed Clusters.
items.abstract: The high GPU demand of ML training makes it hard to allocate large homogeneous clusters of high-end GPUs in a single availability zone. Leveraging heterogeneous GPUs available within and across zones can improve throughput at a reasonable cost. However, training ML models on heterogeneous resources introduces significant challenges, such as stragglers and a large search space of possible job configurations. Current systems lack support for efficiently training models on heterogeneous resources. We present Sailor, a system that automates distributed training over heterogeneous, geo-distributed, and dynamically available resources. Sailor combines an efficient search space exploration algorithm, accurate runtime and memory footprint simulation, and a distributed training framework that supports different types of heterogeneity to optimize training throughput and cost.
items.abstract_zh: ML 训练对 GPU 的高需求使得很难在单个可用区中分配大型同质高端 GPU 集群。利用区域内和跨区域可用的异构 GPU 可以以合理的成本提高吞吐量。然而，在异构资源上训练 ML 模型会带来重大挑战，例如落后者和可能作业配置的巨大搜索空间。当前的系统缺乏对异构资源上有效训练模型的支持。我们推出了 Sailor，这是一个通过异构、地理分布式和动态可用资源自动进行分布式训练的系统。 Sailor 结合了高效的搜索空间探索算法、精确的运行时和内存占用模拟以及支持不同类型异构性的分布式训练框架，以优化训练吞吐量和成本。

items.title: COpter: Efficient Large-Scale Resource-Allocation via Continual Optimization.
items.abstract: Optimization-based resource allocation in large-scale systems often must trade-off responsiveness and allocation quality. Generally, allocations are reconsidered every few minutes (a round ) by formulating and solving a new optimization problem. This paper introduces continual optimization , which reframes round-based resource allocation as a sequence of interconnected problems, leveraging the observation that these resource allocation problems often only change by small amounts across successive rounds to reduce solving times. COpter provides a method for continual optimization of Linear Programs (LP) and Mixed Integer Linear Programs (MILP) formulations of resource allocation problems by combining three innovations: (1) an efficient-to-update problem representation for incremental changes, (2) a proximal-point method implementation that can provably benefit from prior computational effort and allocations, and (3) lightweight heuristics for mixed-integer problems that recover feasible integer solutions with negligible quality loss. We evaluate COpter on problems in three domains: GPU cluster scheduling, shard load balancing, and WAN traffic engineering. Overall, we find that COpter finds high-quality solutions while reducing solver runtimes by 57–83× compared to state-of-the-art commercial solvers. Compared to problem partitioning approaches (POP), COpter simultaneously improves allocation quality and reduces end-to-end allocator runtimes by 1.5–30×.
items.abstract_zh: 大型系统中基于优化的资源分配通常必须权衡响应能力和分配质量。一般来说，每隔几分钟（一轮）就会通过制定和解决新的优化问题来重新考虑分配。本文介绍了持续优化，它将基于轮次的资源分配重新构建为一系列相互关联的问题，利用这些资源分配问题通常在连续轮次中仅发生少量变化的观察结果来减少求解时间。 COpter 通过结合三项创新，提供了一种持续优化资源分配问题的线性规划 (LP) 和混合整数线性规划 (MILP) 公式的方法：(1) 针对增量变化的高效更新问题表示，(2) 可以证明从先前的计算工作和分配中受益的近点方法实现，以及 (3) 针对混合整数问题的轻量级启发式方法，可以在质量损失可忽略不计的情况下恢复可行的整数解。我们针对三个领域的问题评估 COpter：GPU 集群调度、分片负载均衡和 WAN 流量工程。总的来说，我们发现 COpter 找到了高质量的解决方案，同时与最先进的商业求解器相比，求解器运行时间减少了 57-83 倍。与问题划分方法 (POP) 相比，COpter 同时提高了分配质量，并将端到端分配器运行时间缩短了 1.5-30 倍。

items.title: Prove It to the Kernel: Precise Extension Analysis via Proof-Guided Abstraction Refinement.
items.abstract:
Modern OS kernels, such as Linux, employ the eBPF subsystem to enable user space to extend kernel functionality. To ensure safety, an in-kernel verifier statically analyzes these extensions; however, its imprecise analysis frequently results in the erroneous rejection of safe extensions, exposing a critical tension between the precision and computational complexity of the verifier that limits kernel extensibility.

We propose a proof-guided abstraction refinement technique that significantly enhances the verifier's precision while preserving low kernel space complexity. Rather than incorporating sophisticated analysis ( e.g. , via new abstract domains) directly into the verifier, our key insight is to decouple the complex reasoning to user space while bridging the gap through formal proofs. Upon encountering uncertainties, the verifier initiates an abstraction refinement procedure rather than rejecting the extension. As the refinement involves nontrivial reasoning, the verifier simply delineates the task and delegates it to user space. A formal proof is produced externally, which the verifier subsequently checks in linear time before adopting the refined abstraction. Consequently, our approach achieves high precision via user space reasoning while confining kernel space operations to an efficient proof check. Evaluation results show that our technique enables the verifier to accept 403 out of 512 real-world eBPF programs that were previously rejected erroneously, paving the way for more reliable and flexible kernel extensions.
items.abstract_zh:
现代操作系统内核（例如 Linux）采用 eBPF 子系统来使用户空间能够扩展内核功能。为了确保安全，内核验证器对这些扩展进行静态分析；然而，其不精确的分析经常导致错误地拒绝安全扩展，暴露了验证器的精度和计算复杂性之间的关键张力，从而限制了内核的可扩展性。

我们提出了一种以证明为导向的抽象细化技术，可以显着提高验证者的精度，同时保持较低的内核空间复杂性。我们的主要见解不是将复杂的分析（例如，通过新的抽象域）直接合并到验证器中，而是将复杂的推理与用户空间解耦，同时通过形式证明弥合差距。当遇到不确定性时，验证者会启动抽象细化过程，而不是拒绝扩展。由于细化涉及重要的推理，验证者只需描述任务并将其委托给用户空间。外部生成正式证明，验证者随后在采用精炼抽象之前以线性时间对其进行检查。因此，我们的方法通过用户空间推理实现了高精度，同时将内核空间操作限制为有效的证明检查。评估结果表明，我们的技术使验证者能够接受之前被错误拒绝的 512 个真实 eBPF 程序中的 403 个，为更可靠、更灵活的内核扩展铺平了道路。

items.title: Pesto: Cooking up High Performance BFT Queries.
items.abstract: This paper presents Pesto, a high-performance Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) database that offers full SQL compatibility. Pesto intentionally forgoes the use of State Machine Replication (SMR); SMR-based designs offer poor performance due to the several round trips required to order transactions. Pesto, instead, allows for replicas to remain inconsistent, and only synchronizes on demand to ensure that the database remain serializable in the presence of concurrent transactions and malicious actors. On TPC-C, Pesto matches the throughput of Peloton [20] and Postgres [21], two unreplicated SQL database systems, while increasing throughput by 2.3x compared to classic SMR-based BFT-architectures, and reducing latency by 2.7x to 3.9x. Pesto's leaderless design minimizes the impact of replica failures and ensures robust performance.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 Pesto，这是一种高性能拜占庭容错 (BFT) 数据库，提供完全的 SQL 兼容性。 Pesto 有意放弃使用状态机复制 (SMR)；由于排序事务需要多次往返，基于 SMR 的设计性能较差。相反，Pesto 允许副本保持不一致，并且仅按需同步，以确保数据库在存在并发事务和恶意行为者的情况下保持可序列化。在 TPC-C 上，Pesto 与 Peloton [20] 和 Postgres [21] 这两个非复制 SQL 数据库系统的吞吐量相匹配，同时与经典的基于 SMR 的 BFT 架构相比，吞吐量提高了 2.3 倍，并将延迟降低了 2.7 倍到 3.9 倍。 Pesto 的无领导者设计最大限度地减少了副本故障的影响并确保了稳健的性能。

items.title: Robust LLM Training Infrastructure at ByteDance.
items.abstract: The training scale of large language models (LLMs) has reached tens of thousands of GPUs and is still continuously expanding, enabling faster learning of larger models. Accompanying the expansion of the resource scale is the prevalence of failures (CUDA error, NaN values, job hang, etc. ), which poses significant challenges to training stability. Any large-scale LLM training infrastructure should strive for minimal training interruption, efficient fault diagnosis, and effective failure tolerance to enable highly efficient continuous training. This paper presents ByteRobust, a large-scale GPU infrastructure management system tailored for robust and stable training of LLMs. It exploits the uniqueness of LLM training process and gives top priorities to detecting and recovering failures in a routine manner. Leveraging parallelisms and characteristics of LLM training, ByteRobust enables high-capacity fault tolerance, prompt fault demarcation, and localization with an effective data-driven approach, comprehensively ensuring continuous and efficient training of LLM tasks. ByteRobust is deployed on a production GPU platform and advances the state of the art in training robustness by achieving 97% ETTR for a three-month training job on 9,600 GPUs.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）的训练规模已达到数万个 GPU，并且仍在不断扩大，从而能够更快地学习更大的模型。伴随资源规模的扩大，故障（CUDA错误、NaN值、作业挂起等）普遍存在，这对训练稳定性提出了重大挑战。任何大规模的LLM培训基础设施都应该努力实现最小的培训中断、高效的故障诊断和有效的容错，以实现高效的连续培训。本文介绍了 ByteRobust，这是一种专为法学硕士的鲁棒和稳定训练而定制的大规模 GPU 基础设施管理系统。它利用了法学硕士培训过程的独特性，并将以常规方式检测和恢复故障作为首要任务。 ByteRobust利用LLM训练的并行性和特点，通过有效的数据驱动方法实现大容量容错、快速故障定界和定位，全面保障LLM任务训练的持续高效。 ByteRobust 部署在生产 GPU 平台上，通过在 9,600 个 GPU 上进行为期三个月的训练作业实现 97% 的 ETTR，从而提高了训练稳健性的最先进水平。

items.title: PhoenixOS: Concurrent OS-level GPU Checkpoint and Restore with Validated Speculation.
items.abstract:
PhoenixOS (PhOS) is the first OS service that can concurrently checkpoint and restore (C/R) GPU processes—a fundamental capability for critical tasks such as fault tolerance, process migration, and fast startup. While concurrent C/R is well-established on CPUs, it poses unique challenges on GPUs due to their lack of essential features for efficiently tracing concurrent memory reads and writes, such as specific hardware capabilities (e.g., dirty bits) and OS-mediated data paths (e.g., copy-on-write).

To ensure correct concurrent C/R, PhOS proactively detects GPU memory reads and writes through a two-step process: first, it speculates about GPU memory accesses based on the arguments used when launching GPU kernels; then, it validates these accesses efficiently at runtime using binary instrumentation. With this validated speculation, PhOS retrofits CPU-based concurrent C/R for GPUs through software-based approaches, including soft copy-on-write, soft recopy, and soft on-demand restore. PhOS further proposes several GPU-aware techniques for efficient GPU C/R, including coordinated checkpoint data transfer and execution context pool. For downstream tasks that use C/R for tolerating failures, migrating processes between machines, and accelerating cold starts in serverless computing, PhOS achieves orders of magnitude higher performance than state-of-the-art OS-level GPU C/R systems like NVIDIA cuda-checkpoint.
items.abstract_zh:
PhoenixOS (PhOS) 是第一个可以同时检查点和恢复 (C/R) GPU 进程的操作系统服务，这是容错、进程迁移和快速启动等关键任务的基本功能。虽然并发 C/R 在 CPU 上已经很成熟，但它对 GPU 提出了独特的挑战，因为它们缺乏有效跟踪并发内存读写的基本功能，例如特定的硬件功能（例如脏位）和操作系统介导的数据路径（例如写入时复制）。

为了确保正确的并发 C/R，PhOS 通过两步过程主动检测 GPU 内存读写：首先，它根据启动 GPU 内核时使用的参数推测 GPU 内存访问；然后，它使用二进制检测在运行时有效地验证这些访问。根据这一经过验证的推测，PhOS 通过基于软件的方法（包括写入时软复制、软重新复制和软按需恢复）改进了 GPU 的基于 CPU 的并发 C/R。 PhOS 进一步提出了几种用于高效 GPU C/R 的 GPU 感知技术，包括协调检查点数据传输和执行上下文池。对于使用 C/R 来容忍故障、在机器之间迁移进程以及加速无服务器计算中的冷启动的下游任务，PhOS 的性能比 NVIDIA cuda-checkpoint 等最先进的操作系统级 GPU C/R 系统高出几个数量级。

items.title: Aegaeon: Effective GPU Pooling for Concurrent LLM Serving on the Market.
items.abstract:
Model markets ( e.g. , Hugging Face) feature a wide variety of models with unique characteristics and varying levels of popularity. Serving sporadic and unpredictable requests in concurrent inference workloads with dedicated GPU instances results in substantial resource waste. While existing multi-model serving solutions use GPU pooling and server-less computing to improve resource efficiency, their effective-ness is limited to supporting at most two or three models per GPU, which is inadequate for fully utilizing GPU resources.

We propose Aegaeon, a multi-model serving system that performs model auto-scaling at the token granularity to achieve effective GPU pooling. Aegaeon schedules multimodel requests and makes auto-scaling decisions on a per-token basis to maximize service quality. It reduces auto-scaling overhead by 97% through component reuse, explicit memory management, and fine-grained KV cache synchronization. Experiments show that Aegaeon sustains 2–2.5× higher request arrival rates or 1.5–9× more goodput compared to existing solutions. Aegaeon has been beta deployed in our model marketplace and currently serves tens of models. Deployment results show that Aegaeon reduces the number of GPUs required for serving these models from 1,192 to 213, highlighting an 82% GPU resource saving.
items.abstract_zh:
模特市场（例如，Hugging Face）拥有各种各样的模特，这些模特具有独特的特征和不同的受欢迎程度。使用专用 GPU 实例在并发推理工作负载中处理零星且不可预测的请求会导致大量资源浪费。现有的多模型服务解决方案虽然利用GPU池化和无服务器计算来提高资源效率，但其有效性仅限于每个GPU最多支持两个或三个模型，这不足以充分利用GPU资源。

我们提出了 Aegaeon，一种多模型服务系统，可以在 token 粒度上执行模型自动缩放，以实现有效的 GPU 池化。 Aegaeon 安排多模型请求并根据每个令牌做出自动扩展决策，以最大限度地提高服务质量。它通过组件重用、显式内存管理和细粒度 KV 缓存同步，将自动扩展开销减少了 97%。实验表明，与现有解决方案相比，Aegaeon 的请求到达率提高了 2-2.5 倍，吞吐量提高了 1.5-9 倍。 Aegaeon 已在我们的模型市场中进行测试部署，目前为数十种模型提供服务。部署结果显示，Aegaeon 将服务这些模型所需的 GPU 数量从 1,192 个减少到 213 个，显着节省了 82% 的 GPU 资源。

items.title: KNighter: Transforming Static Analysis with LLM-Synthesized Checkers.
items.abstract:
Static analysis is a powerful technique for bug detection in critical systems like operating system kernels. However, designing and implementing static analyzers is challenging, time-consuming, and typically limited to predefined bug patterns. While large language models (LLMs) have shown promise for static analysis, directly applying them to scan large systems remains impractical due to computational constraints and contextual limitations.

We present KNighter, the first approach that unlocks scalable LLM-based static analysis by automatically synthesizing static analyzers from historical bug patterns. Rather than using LLMs to directly analyze massive systems, our key insight is leveraging LLMs to generate specialized static analyzers guided by historical patch knowledge. KNighter implements this vision through a multi-stage synthesis pipeline that validates checker correctness against original patches and employs an automated refinement process to iteratively reduce false positives. Our evaluation on the Linux kernel demonstrates that KNighter generates high-precision checkers capable of detecting diverse bug patterns overlooked by existing human-written analyzers. To date, KNighter-synthesized checkers have discovered 92 new, critical, longlatent bugs (average 4.3 years) in the Linux kernel; 77 are confirmed, 57 fixed, and 30 have been assigned CVE numbers. This work establishes an entirely new paradigm for scalable, reliable, and traceable LLM-based static analysis for real-world systems via checker synthesis.
items.abstract_zh:
静态分析是一种强大的技术，用于检测操作系统内核等关键系统中的错误。然而，设计和实现静态分析器具有挑战性、耗时，并且通常仅限于预定义的错误模式。虽然大型语言模型 (LLM) 已显示出静态分析的前景，但由于计算限制和上下文限制，直接应用它们来扫描大型系统仍然不切实际。

我们推出 KNighter，这是第一种通过从历史 bug 模式自动合成静态分析器来解锁可扩展的基于 LLM 的静态分析的方法。我们的关键见解是利用 LLM 生成由历史补丁知识引导的专门静态分析器，而不是使用 LLM 直接分析大型系统。 KNighter 通过多阶段综合管道实现这一愿景，该管道根据原始补丁验证检查器的正确性，并采用自动细化过程来迭代减少误报。我们对 Linux 内核的评估表明，KNighter 生成的高精度检查器能够检测现有人工编写的分析器忽略的各种错误模式。迄今为止，KNighter 合成的检查器已在 Linux 内核中发现了 92 个新的、严重的、长期潜伏的错误（平均 4.3 年）； 77 个已确认，57 个已修复，30 个已分配 CVE 编号。这项工作通过检查器合成为现实系统建立了一个全新的范例，用于可扩展、可靠和可追踪的基于 LLM 的静态分析。

items.title: SAND: A New Programming Abstraction for Video-based Deep Learning.
items.abstract:
Video-based deep learning (VDL) is increasingly used across diverse applications and has become highly popular, but it faces significant challenges in preprocessing highly compressed video data. Preprocessing pipelines are complex, requiring extensive engineering effort, and introduce computational bottlenecks, with latency exceeding GPU training time. Existing solutions partially mitigate these issues but remain inefficient and resource-constrained.

We present SAND, a framework for VDL that integrates system-level optimizations to simplify the preprocessing pipeline and maximize resource efficiency. First, SAND introduces a view abstraction that encapsulates key preprocessing stages into virtualized objects, eliminating the need for users to manage individual objects. Second, SAND maximizes reuse opportunities through efficient system-level object management, reducing the preprocessing overhead and improving GPU utilization. Evaluation across multiple VDL applications and diverse environments, including Ray-based hyperparameter search and distributed data parallel training, shows GPU utilization improvements of up to 12.3× and 2.9× over CPU and GPU baselines, respectively, while reducing preprocessing code complexity from hundreds or thousands of lines to fewer than 10.
items.abstract_zh:
基于视频的深度学习（VDL）越来越多地在各种应用中使用，并且变得非常流行，但它在预处理高度压缩的视频数据方面面临着巨大的挑战。预处理管道很复杂，需要大量的工程工作，并且会引入计算瓶颈，延迟超过 GPU 训练时间。现有的解决方案部分缓解了这些问题，但仍然效率低下且资源有限。

我们提出了 SAND，这是一个 VDL 框架，它集成了系统级优化，以简化预处理管道并最大限度地提高资源效率。首先，SAND 引入了视图抽象，将关键预处理阶段封装到虚拟化对象中，从而消除了用户管理单个对象的需要。其次，SAND 通过高效的系统级对象管理最大限度地提高重用机会，减少预处理开销并提高 GPU 利用率。对多个 VDL 应用程序和不同环境（包括基于 Ray 的超参数搜索和分布式数据并行训练）的评估显示，与 CPU 和 GPU 基线相比，GPU 利用率分别提高了 12.3 倍和 2.9 倍，同时将预处理代码复杂性从数百或数千行降低到不到 10 行。

items.title: IC-Cache: Efficient Large Language Model Serving via In-context Caching.
items.abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) have excelled in various applications, yet serving them at scale is challenging due to their substantial resource demands and high latency. Our real-world studies reveal that over 70% of user requests to LLMs have semantically similar counterparts, suggesting the potential for knowledge transfer among requests. However, naively caching and reusing past responses leads to a big quality drop.

In this paper, we introduce IC-Cache, a caching system that enables live LLM capability augmentation to improve serving efficiency: by leveraging historical request-response pairs from larger models as in-context examples, IC-Cache empowers small LLMs to imitate and even exceed the compositional abilities (e.g., reasoning) of their larger counterparts, enabling selective offloading of requests to reduce cost and latency. Achieving this live augmentation at scale introduces intricate trade-offs between response quality, latency, and system throughput. For a new request, IC-Cache efficiently selects similar, high-utility examples to prepend them to the new request's input. At scale, it adaptively routes requests across LLMs of varying capabilities, accounting for response quality and serving loads. IC-Cache employs a cost-aware cache replay mechanism that refines example quality offline to maximize online cache utility and efficiency. Evaluations on millions of realistic requests demonstrate that IC-Cache improves LLM serving throughput by 1.4–5.9x and reduces latency by 28–71% without hurting response quality.
items.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型 (LLM) 在各种应用程序中表现出色，但由于其大量的资源需求和高延迟，大规模为其提供服务具有挑战性。我们的现实世界研究表明，超过 70% 的 LLM 用户请求具有语义相似的对应项，这表明请求之间存在知识转移的潜力。然而，天真地缓存和重用过去的响应会导致质量大幅下降。

在本文中，我们介绍了 IC-Cache，这是一种能够增强实时 LLM 能力以提高服务效率的缓存系统：通过利用较大模型的历史请求响应对作为上下文示例，IC-Cache 使小型 LLM 能够模仿甚至超越其较大对应模型的组合能力（例如推理），从而能够选择性卸载请求以降低成本和延迟。大规模实现这种实时增强需要在响应质量、延迟和系统吞吐量之间进行复杂的权衡。对于新请求，IC-Cache 会有效地选择类似的、高效用的示例，将它们添加到新请求的输入之前。在规模上，它可以在不同能力的法学硕士之间自适应地路由请求，同时考虑响应质量和服务负载。 IC-Cache 采用成本感知型缓存重放机制，可提高离线示例质量，从而最大限度地提高在线缓存效用和效率。对数百万个实际请求的评估表明，IC-Cache 将 LLM 服务吞吐量提高了 1.4–5.9 倍，并将延迟降低了 28–71%，而不会损害响应质量。

items.title: Quilt: Resource-aware Merging of Serverless Workflows.
items.abstract: This paper describes Quilt, a serverless optimizer that automatically merges workflows that consist of many functions (possibly in different languages) into one process thereby avoiding high invocation latency, communication overhead, and long chains of cold starts. Instead of merging all functions, Quilt takes into account the provider's resource constraints to decide which functions to merge. Quilt is compatible with existing platforms without modification (Fission, OpenWhisk, and OpenFaaS), can merge functions in different languages (C, C++, Swift, Go, Rust) by acting at the level of LLVM IR, and requires no input or help from developers. Our evaluation shows that Quilt improves median workflow completion time by 45.63%–70.95% and throughput by 2.05×–12.87×.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 Quilt，一种无服务器优化器，它自动将由许多函数（可能采用不同语言）组成的工作流合并到一个进程中，从而避免高调用延迟、通信开销和长冷启动链。 Quilt 不是合并所有函数，而是考虑提供者的资源限制来决定合并哪些函数。 Quilt 无需修改即可兼容现有平台（Fission、OpenWhisk 和 OpenFaaS），可以通过作用于 LLVM IR 级别来合并不同语言（C、C++、Swift、Go、Rust）的功能，并且不需要开发人员的输入或帮助。我们的评估表明，Quilt 将工作流程中值完成时间提高了 45.63%–70.95%，吞吐量提高了 2.05×–12.87×。

items.title: Jenga: Effective Memory Management for Serving LLM with Heterogeneity.
items.abstract:
Large language models are widely used but expensive to run. To reduce costs, it is crucial to maximize request batch size through efficient GPU memory management. Existing approaches, such as PagedAttention, struggle with modern LLMs because of the growing heterogeneity in the sizes of models' internal embeddings and attention mechanisms.

In this paper, we present Jenga, a memory allocation framework for these heterogeneous LLMs. Jenga tackles two key challenges: (1) memory fragmentation caused by embeddings of different sizes, and (2) unpredictable memory usage from varying attention mechanisms across layers. Jenga employs an attention-property-aware allocator, leveraging the least common multiple (LCM) of embedding sizes to optimize memory usage and performing cache eviction based on attention patterns to enhance memory reuse. We implement Jenga in vLLM, and evaluate it with diverse LLMs, datasets, and GPUs. Evaluations show that Jenga improves GPU memory utilization by up to 83% and serving throughput by up to 2.16× (1.46× on average).
items.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型被广泛使用，但运行成本昂贵。为了降低成本，通过高效的 GPU 内存管理来最大化请求批量大小至关重要。由于模型内部嵌入和注意力机制的大小日益增长的异质性，现有的方法（例如 PagedAttention）很难与现代法学硕士相媲美。

在本文中，我们提出 Jenga，一种用于这些异构 LLM 的内存分配框架。 Jenga 解决了两个关键挑战：（1）不同大小的嵌入导致的内存碎片，以及（2）跨层不同的注意力机制导致的不可预测的内存使用情况。 Jenga 采用注意力属性感知分配器，利用嵌入大小的最小公倍数 (LCM) 来优化内存使用，并根据注意力模式执行缓存驱逐以增强内存重用。我们在 vLLM 中实现 Jenga，并使用不同的 LLM、数据集和 GPU 对其进行评估。评估显示，Jenga 将 GPU 内存利用率提高高达 83%，服务吞吐量提高高达 2.16 倍（平均 1.46 倍）。

items.title: DiffKV: Differentiated Memory Management for Large Language Models with Parallel KV Compaction.
items.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable capabilities but face substantial serving costs due to their high memory demands, with the key-value (KV) cache being a primary bottleneck. State-of-the-art KV cache compression techniques, such as quantization and pruning, apply uniform treatment to both keys and values, and discard unimportant tokens entirely, overlooking the fine-grained distinctions in the significance of individual KV cache components. To address such limitations, we introduce DiffKV , a novel framework for efficient KV cache compression that exploits three levels of differentiation in the KV cache: (1) the differing impact of keys and values on attention computation, (2) the varying importance of tokens, and (3) the diverse dynamic sparsity patterns across attention heads. These levels of differentiation introduce irregular memory usage patterns across different requests and attention heads, posing significant scalability challenges for memory management. To address these challenges, DiffKV proposes an on-GPU memory manager that compacts fragmented free memory list into contiguous regions in parallel, effectively translating sparsity in the KV cache into performance gains. We evaluate DiffKV on several mainstream LLMs, including the emerging thinking models that generate extended chains of thought. DiffKV is able to compress the KV cache by 2.7× to 5.7× with near-lossless accuracy on complex workloads requiring sophisticated reasoning and long-generation capabilities, and enhances throughput by 1.9× to 5.4×.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 展示了卓越的功能，但由于其高内存需求而面临巨大的服务成本，其中键值 (KV) 缓存是主要瓶颈。最先进的 KV 缓存压缩技术，例如量化和剪枝，对键和值进行统一处理，并完全丢弃不重要的标记，忽略了各个 KV 缓存组件重要性的细粒度区别。为了解决这些限制，我们引入了 DiffKV，这是一种高效 KV 缓存压缩的新颖框架，它利用 KV 缓存中的三个级别的差异：（1）键和值对注意力计算的不同影响，（2）令牌的不同重要性，以及（3）注意力头之间的不同动态稀疏模式。这些级别的差异在不同的请求和注意力头中引入了不规则的内存使用模式，给内存管理带来了重大的可扩展性挑战。为了应对这些挑战，DiffKV 提出了一种 GPU 内存管理器，它将碎片化的空闲内存列表并行压缩到连续区域，有效地将 KV 缓存中的稀疏性转化为性能增益。我们在几个主流法学硕士上评估了 DiffKV，包括产生扩展思维链的新兴思维模型。 DiffKV 能够在需要复杂推理和长生成能力的复杂工作负载上以近乎无损的精度将 KV 缓存压缩 2.7 倍至 5.7 倍，并将吞吐量提高 1.9 倍至 5.4 倍。

items.title: CortenMM: Efficient Memory Management with Strong Correctness Guarantees.
items.abstract:
Modern memory management systems suffer from poor performance and subtle concurrency bugs, slowing down applications while introducing security vulnerabilities. We observe that both issues stem from the conventional design of memory management systems with two levels of abstraction: a software-level abstraction ( e.g. , VMA trees in Linux) and a hardware-level abstraction (typically, page tables). This design increases portability but requires correctly and efficiently synchronizing two drastically different and complex data structures, which is generally challenging.

We present CortenMM, a memory management system with a clean-slate design to achieve both high performance and synchronization correctness. Our key insight is that most OSes no longer need the software-level abstraction, since mainstream ISAs use nearly identical hardware MMU formats. Therefore, departing from prior designs, CortenMM eliminates the software-level abstraction to achieve sweeping simplicity. Exploiting this simplicity, CortenMM proposes a transactional interface with scalable locking protocols to program the MMU, achieving high performance by avoiding the extra contention in the software-level abstraction. The one-level design further enables us to formally verify the correctness of concurrent code operating on the MMU (correctness of basic operations and locking protocols), thereby offering strong correctness guarantees. Our evaluation shows that the formally verified CortenMM outperforms Linux by 1.2× to 26× on real-world applications.
items.abstract_zh:
现代内存管理系统存在性能差和微妙的并发错误，降低了应用程序的速度，同时引入了安全漏洞。我们观察到这两个问题都源于具有两个抽象级别的内存管理系统的传统设计：软件级抽象（例如 Linux 中的 VMA 树）和硬件级抽象（通常是页表）。这种设计提高了可移植性，但需要正确有效地同步两个截然不同且复杂的数据结构，这通常具有挑战性。

我们推出 CortenMM，这是一种采用全新设计的内存管理系统，可实现高性能和同步正确性。我们的主要见解是，大多数操作系统不再需要软件级抽象，因为主流 ISA 使用几乎相同的硬件 MMU 格式。因此，与之前的设计不同，CortenMM 消除了软件级抽象，以实现彻底的简单性。利用这种简单性，CortenMM 提出了一种具有可扩展锁定协议的事务接口来对 MMU 进行编程，通过避免软件级抽象中的额外争用来实现高性能。一级设计进一步使我们能够形式化地验证在MMU上运行的并发代码的正确性（基本操作和锁定协议的正确性），从而提供强有力的正确性保证。我们的评估表明，经过正式验证的 CortenMM 在实际应用中的性能优于 Linux 1.2 倍到 26 倍。

items.title: AutoMan: Facilitating Verified Distributed Systems Development Through Automatic Code Generation and Manual Optimizations.
items.abstract: Developing correct and performant distributed systems is notoriously challenging due to their complexity and scale. There are two main approaches to addressing correctness issues that stem from their complexity: (i) formal verification , and (ii) automatic compilation of specifications to implementations. The former provides machine-checked correctness guarantees along with good performance but requires substantial expert effort. In contrast, the latter can reduce developer effort, though often at the expense of rigorous correctness guarantees. In this paper, we design, develop, and evaluate the AutoMan workflow, which makes developing distributed systems with refinement-based formal verification techniques more accessible and practical for both experts and developers. AutoMan achieves this by automatically generating implementations and their corresponding verification obligations from formal system specifications. This is accomplished without placing trust in the code generator and without sacrificing end-to-end correctness or performance. AutoMan's use of refinement-based verification methodology for ensuring soundness allows hand-tuned performance-critical code and automatically generated code to harmoniously co-exist without jeopardizing end-to-end correctness guarantees. The effectiveness of AutoMan is demonstrated through the reimplementation of Multi-Paxos, PBFT, a sharded Key-Value store, and CausalMesh following the AutoMan methodology. In all cases, the use of AutoMan substantially reduced development effort (e.g., 70%-97% for Multi-Paxos), while the resulting systems maintained robust efficiency and correctness.
items.abstract_zh: 由于其复杂性和规模，开发正确且高性能的分布式系统是众所周知的挑战。有两种主要方法可以解决源于其复杂性的正确性问题：（i）形式验证，以及（ii）将规范自动编译为实现。前者提供机器检查的正确性保证以及良好的性能，但需要大量的专家努力。相比之下，后者可以减少开发人员的工作量，尽管通常会牺牲严格的正确性保证。在本文中，我们设计、开发和评估 AutoMan 工作流程，这使得专家和开发人员都更容易使用基于细化的形式验证技术来开发分布式系统。 AutoMan 通过根据正式系统规范自动生成实现及其相应的验证义务来实现这一目标。这是在不信任代码生成器的情况下完成的，也不会牺牲端到端的正确性或性能。 AutoMan 使用基于细化的验证方法来确保可靠性，允许手动调整的性能关键代码和自动生成的代码和谐共存，而不会危及端到端的正确性保证。 AutoMan 的有效性是通过遵循 AutoMan 方法论重新实现 Multi-Paxos、PBFT、分片键值存储和 CausalMesh 来证明的。在所有情况下，AutoMan 的使用都大大减少了开发工作量（例如，Multi-Paxos 减少了 70%-97%），而最终的系统保持了稳健的效率和正确性。

items.title: Oasis: Pooling PCIe Devices Over CXL to Boost Utilization.
items.abstract: PCIe devices, such as NICs and SSDs, are frequently underutilized in cloud platforms. PCIe device pools, in which multiple hosts can share a set of PCIe devices, could increase PCIe device utilization and reduce their total cost of ownership. The main way to achieve PCIe device pools today is via PCIe switches, but they are expensive and inflexible. We design Oasis, 1 a system that pools PCIe devices in software over CXL memory pools. CXL memory pools are already being deployed to boost datacenter memory utilization and reduce costs. Once CXL pools are in place, they can serve as an efficient data path between hosts and PCIe devices. Oasis provides a control plane and datapath over CXL pools, mapping and routing PCIe device traffic across host boundaries. PCIe devices with different functionalities can be supported by adding an Oasis engine for each device class. We implement an Oasis network engine to demonstrate NIC pooling. Our evaluation shows that Oasis improves the NIC utilization by 2× and handles NIC failover with only a 38 ms interruption.
items.abstract_zh: PCIe 设备（例如 NIC 和 SSD）在云平台中经常未得到充分利用。 PCIe 设备池（其中多个主机可以共享一组 PCIe 设备）可以提高 PCIe 设备利用率并降低总体拥有成本。目前实现 PCIe 设备池的主要方式是通过 PCIe 交换机，但它们价格昂贵且不灵活。我们设计了 Oasis，1 一个通过 CXL 内存池在软件中汇集 PCIe 设备的系统。 CXL 内存池已被部署，以提高数据中心内存利用率并降低成本。一旦 CXL 池就位，它们就可以充当主机和 PCIe 设备之间的高效数据路径。 Oasis 在 CXL 池上提供控制平面和数据路径，跨主机边界映射和路由 PCIe 设备流量。通过为每个设备类别添加 Oasis 引擎，可以支持具有不同功能的 PCIe 设备。我们实现了 Oasis 网络引擎来演示 NIC 池。我们的评估表明，Oasis 将 NIC 利用率提高了 2 倍，并且处理 NIC 故障转移时仅出现 38 毫秒的中断。

items.title: Sleeping with One Eye Open: Fast, Sustainable Storage with Sandman.
items.abstract: All-flash servers, while being widely popular for their high performance and large capacity, can incur significant energy consumption in modern storage systems. Through a motivational study, we discover that the culprit is the inefficiency in the software stack, and existing power-saving methods fail to deliver comparable performance, especially under workload bursts. Guided by the lessons learned, we propose Sandman, a scheduling framework that combines the fast resource scaling mechanism, resource monitoring, and I/O burst detection policies. Experiments show that Sandman reduces average power consumption by up to 39.38% and energy consumption by up to 33.36% while delivering performance comparable (within 5% in corner cases) to the best performance case (the busy-polling stack) in both benchmarks and field workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 全闪存服务器虽然因其高性能和大容量而广受欢迎，但在现代存储系统中也会产生大量的能源消耗。通过动机研究，我们发现罪魁祸首是软件堆栈的低效率，而现有的节能方法无法提供可比的性能，尤其是在工作负载突发的情况下。在经验教训的指导下，我们提出了Sandman，一个结合了快速资源扩展机制、资源监控和I/O突发检测策略的调度框架。实验表明，Sandman 将平均功耗降低了 39.38%，能耗降低了 33.36%，同时在基准测试和现场工作负载中提供了与最佳性能情况（繁忙轮询堆栈）相当的性能（在极端情况下在 5% 以内）。

items.title: cache_ext: Customizing the Page Cache with eBPF.
items.abstract: The OS page cache is central to the performance of many applications, by reducing excessive accesses to storage. However, its one-size-fits-all eviction policy performs poorly in many workloads. While the systems community has experimented with a plethora of new and adaptive eviction policies in non-OS settings (e.g., key-value stores, CDNs), it is very difficult to implement such policies in the page cache, due to the complexity of modifying kernel code. To address these shortcomings, we design a flexible eBPF-based framework for the Linux page cache, called cache_ext, that allows developers to customize the page cache without modifying the kernel. cache_ext enables applications to customize the page cache policy for their specific needs, while also ensuring that different applications' policies do not interfere with each other and preserving the page cache's ability to share memory across different processes. We demonstrate the flexibility of cache_ext's interface by using it to implement eight different policies, including sophisticated eviction algorithms. Our evaluation shows that it is indeed beneficial for applications to customize the page cache to match their workloads' unique properties, and that they can achieve up to 70% higher throughput and 58% lower tail latency.
items.abstract_zh: 操作系统页面缓存通过减少对存储的过度访问而成为许多应用程序性能的核心。然而，其一刀切的驱逐策略在许多工作负载中表现不佳。虽然系统社区在非操作系统设置（例如键值存储、CDN）中尝试了大量新的自适应逐出策略，但由于修改内核代码的复杂性，在页面缓存中实现此类策略非常困难。为了解决这些缺点，我们为Linux页面缓存设计了一个灵活的基于eBPF的框架，称为cache_ext，它允许开发人员在不修改内核的情况下自定义页面缓存。 cache_ext 使应用程序能够根据其特定需求自定义页面缓存策略，同时还确保不同应用程序的策略不会相互干扰，并保留页面缓存在不同进程之间共享内存的能力。我们通过使用它来实现八种不同的策略（包括复杂的逐出算法）来展示cache_ext接口的灵活性。我们的评估表明，应用程序自定义页面缓存以匹配其工作负载的独特属性确实是有益的，并且它们可以实现高达 70% 的吞吐量提高和 58% 的尾部延迟降低。
